programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dr Sloan worries that his neighbours have something to hide, and is also concerned with Steve, who appears to be falling for a woman who bears an uncanny resemblance to his murdered girlfriend

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A film-maker begins using Community General Hospital as the location for an eerie documentary - and it is not long before Dr Sloan discovers the building may genuinely be haunted

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble must testify to save an ex-policeman from a murder charge, at the risk of revealing his identity in the process

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: When ruthless oil tycoon Jerome Klee is murdered, the evidence points to a foreman who tried to keep him from tricking a ranch owner out of a big payout

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The hero goes undercover to bust a casino's money-laundering scheme, but is knocked unconscious. Walker is left running from the Mob - confused as to which side of the law he is on

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the case of a skyscraper climber who plummeted from the Empire State Building. Returning to the floor from which he fell, Mac and Flack discover the dead man may have witnessed a murder. Meanwhile, Stella and Danny look into the killing of a fashion designer

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team looks into a raid on a naval base, and Dr Cyril Taft - the surgeon who saved Gibbs' life - experiences the thrill of assisting on an investigation. Assigned to treat Gibbs' suspect, Taft manages to uncover evidence that may prove crucial to solving the case. Meanwhile, DiNozzo pays his old flame Jeanne Benoit a visit as he chases a lead involving doctors in Sudan. Guest starring John Cryer and Scottie Thompson

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. Kensi is taken into custody as chief suspect in the murder of Marine Sergeant Chris Black, but fancies her chances of catching the real killer herself. Meanwhile, Callen and the rest of the team work to prove their colleague's innocence. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: In a corrupt town, Richard Kimble is savagely beaten by two corrupt policemen who are under orders of the one-armed man

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry is accused of bribing a witness, but further investigation leads to a disreputable Cockney sea merchant who looks eerily like the noted attorney

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs discovers a link between the murder of a petty officer and the break-up of his team, leading him and new director Leon Vance to uncover a case of blackmail involving an NCIS agent. The duo single out three suspects, but learn vital information has been stolen, and realise the whole Persian Gulf Fleet could be in danger

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A wealthy senator threatens to disband the Rangers because of budget cutbacks

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. Kensi goes in search of her father's killer, before bumping into her mother, who she has not seen for more than 15 years. Meanwhile, the discovery that a member of Kensi's father's unit faked his own death prompts Granger to back down from his suspicions of his colleague. Crime drama, starring Daniela Ruah and Miguel Ferrer

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A lieutenant apparently commits suicide by jumping from an aircraft carrier and Ziva and McGee are dispatched to inform the widow - but arrive to find that she has been killed. Subsequent inquiries reveal that the injuries on her body were inflicted after death and the murder scene was carefully staged to mislead investigators. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Cote de Pablo

programme.desc: A day spent working on a cut-and-dried case leaves the team free to spend some time away from work, and while McGee and Delilah argue over the importance of keeping shop-talk to a minimum, DiNozzo ends up boring his dinner date with crime-solving tales. However, the agents' personal plans are all interrupted as, one-by-one, they realise errors have been made in closing their latest investigation. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: The team is forced to establish whether the gunning down of a man outside the Vietnamese Consulate is an isolated incident or a terrorist attack, facing a race against time to ensure the safety of delegates at an upcoming international conference. Crime drama, guest starring Dan Lauria (The Wonder Years)

programme.desc: The fate of John Wilkes Booth, the man who assassinated President Abraham Lincoln on April 14, 1865. programme.desc: Business students go wild at a bizarre festival near Valencia, Spain, where 25,000 people gather annually to pelt each other with tomatoes. programme.title: Airline
programme.desc: News of the September 11 terrorist attacks reaches Luton, and it is not long before airport supervisor Leo Jones is besieged by anxious passengers seeking further information. programme.title: Airline
programme.desc: Fog descends on Luton Airport, ensuring new boy Leo Jones has his hands full dealing with the disruption. programme.desc: Nora starts a cleaning job at the home of a much-praised widower across the valley, leaving Compo writhing in jealousy. Long-running comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard secretly buys a van and asks Wesley to customise it for long journeys, but Compo insists on pushing the vehicle to its limits. Truly thinks Clegg is paying too high a tax, so he drags his friend to a council meeting. Auntie Wainwright has Smiler deliver a truck load of junk that the recipient did not order. Peter Sallis and Howard Fyfe star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When a man is found dead inside a locked bank vault, Father Brown must solve the mystery to save an innocent man from paying the ultimate price. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as a Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest finds himself in a seemingly impossible situation when he is told during a confession that Bishop Talbot will be assassinated tomorrow. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Standing loses £10,000 in a high-stakes poker game - but his opponent, a bookmaker, agrees to write off the debt if the detective can bring his father's killer to justice. Lane helps him uncover a connection with the case of a champion greyhound abducted in the 1980s, and a map comes to light that appears to lead to the missing dog's grave. Meanwhile, a widow mounts a protest outside the station

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A shocking incident on a suburban street brings back painful memories for Jack, forcing him to revisit his past. A member of the victim's family seeks justice of their own.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: New Sun Hill team member Sgt Rachel Weston (Claire Goose) joins forces with Mickey and Mel to investigate links between a road traffic accident and a break-in at a jewellery shop. The getaway van abandoned at the scene of the crash is traced to a squatter couple, who deny any involvement in the crime and claim they were looking after the 12-year-old passenger after finding him begging on the streets

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A London banker who is an old school friend of James comes to stay for a long weekend, and gives him advice about going into partnership with Granville Bennett. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford hears how some missing parcels can end up being sold off at auction instead of safely delivered. Plus, the rural communities who struggled to get quality broadband

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores an ambitious housing development in Tredegar, Dion Dublin visits a house in Great Barr and Martel Maxwell is impressed by a six-bedroom terrace in Liverpool

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works with one of the UK's biggest water companies. He traces the flow of this precious resource from the reservoir to homes and businesses, before finding out what happens with wastewater at the other end of the system

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A father braves a house fire to save his daughter. A pregnant woman on the way to hospital does not make it, so her partner dials 999 when the baby begins to arrive in the car

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as two teams search for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, led by experts Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks. Christina learns about 'The Leominster Chelsea Bun', which travelled to the battle fields of the First World War and back

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie faces a choice - does she want to a medical career or a life of showbusiness glitz and glamour? A young carer struggles to cope when his grandma's obsession with an old music hall performance gets out of hand

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists screen print designer Daniel Heath and clock restorer Paul Parry to rejuvenate an old writing bureau and a 1940s mantle clock. She also tries to cultivate something sellable from fragrant bags of garden waste

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park code breaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered old Victorian firefighter's helmet. A threadbare teddy on wheels is also restored

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Greg McKenzie meets young people born or brought up in the UK who say Home Office immigration policy treats them as second class citizens. They cannot vote, cannot claim benefits, struggle to go to university and may even face deportation. Panorama investigates Home Office policy which gives them a chance to stay in the UK, but which takes 10 years and costs thousands of pounds

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. Kirsten Ramsay is called upon to painstakingly piece together a vintage ceramic night light, fractured into dozens of fragments, and skateboard enthusiast Mike is hoping Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea can restore his wooden board, which transports him back to his youth in 1960s Liverpool. Finally, toy restorers Julie and Amanda undertake the repair of a treasured teddy bear that has been by the side of a young man as he's undergone life-changing surgery

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants go global, taking on garments and techniques from around the world. For the pattern challenge they make a French breton top - and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly matched stripes and a completely flat neck - before they transform sarongs into completely different garments. In the final round, the sewers must come up with an outfit inspired by Mexican artist Frida Kahlo, known as much for her personal style as for her vivid artwork. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Unsolved: An Alibi for Omar?
programme.desc: Journalist Bronagh Munro uncovers new evidence casting doubt on the conviction of a man who has spent 19 years in prison for murder. Omar Benguit was jailed for killing 26-year-old Korean student Jong-Ok Shin in Bournemouth in 2002. Now, Bronagh tracks down some of the alleged witnesses to ask them what they remember. She also finds evidence that could give Omar Benguit an alibi for the night of the murder

programme.title: Boxing: Canelo Alvarez
programme.desc: A profile of the Mexican boxer, who has won world titles in four different weight classes

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Fleur East, as they assist two friends from London in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing acrobat, a princess and a karate champion

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville meet the devoted dog owners who insured their precious pets only to find out it didn't pay out when disaster struck. Julia also hears from people looking for love who found themselves shelling out more than they expected with one online dating site

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a property in Southampton in a stunning setting, right beside the river, and another in Wellington, Shropshire being renovated with family very much in mind. Plus, a revisit to Clay Cross, Derbyshire, where in October 2018 Dion Dublin saw a plot of land with exciting development potential

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works in construction, finding out what it takes to join the men and women who are building Britain. Around 2.4 million people work in the construction industry, making it one of the largest employers in the UK - worth over £100billion pounds in 2019 alone

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: More stories of near misses, featuring a teenage girl crashing into a bridge during a go-kart race, while her anxious parents watch in the stands. Plus, a kayaking mother and daughter stranded on mud flats after getting caught out by the tide. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe guide two teams scouring Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire for bargains to take to auction. The reds show David how to be cool, while the blues go in search of a duck-billed platypus. Plus, presenter Eric Knowles pays a visit to the Museum of Timekeeping in nearby Upton to find out about a local man's mission to save the St Pancras Railway Clock

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie continues on her quest, but a surprise awaits as she follows her next lead. Tensions run high as the Mill Team faces formidable competition on the sports pitch

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Isle of Arran to house-hunt with a novelist and her husband who are in search of writing inspiration from the Scottish wilderness for their £375,000 budget. Along the way, Jules discovers the positive benefits to Arran's marine habitats and the local fishing industry since Scotland's first 'No Take Zone' was introduced

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph goes on the lookout for quality discards at Walsall Recycling Centre, in the West Midlands. There are pine kitchen units to be transformed with the help of carpenter Bruce Faulseit and some life jackets and water skiing ropes that bag-maker Rosanna Gethin hopes to make shipshape. Jacqui herself sets to work on a pair of mid-century dining chairs

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea welcomes 93-year-old Dora Paul to the workshop, along with the unusual item she's brought in for repair - an old tram seat that once ferried passengers on the number 81 tram in Salford. A vintage horse-racing game and two stained-glass panels also receive attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones co-hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen is asked to record a TV appeal for Bailey but her fears get the better of her - and to make matters worse, nasty comments on the fundraising page threaten to send her over the edge. Phil insists that Callum fix things between him and Ben, Mick has a decision to make, and Dotty disapproves of Sonia's gesture

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Self-made millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies observe a pitch by a plucky entrepreneur with lofty ambitions for his genetically personalised meal shakes. A married couple hope their alcoholic gift box business will make for an intoxicating proposition and the Dragons' are put to the test with a virtual escape room business - will they unlock their coffers and strike a deal? Finally, a 'head nit-picker' is itching to show off her headlice treatment and clinic. Whether the Dragons share her passion for parasites remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the homeowners whose garden sheds have been left facing the axe, due to their local council's planning regulations. Julia also meets a woman whose act of charity was scuppered when her delivery company lost the parcels she was sending to help others

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a two-bedroom flat in Grays in Essex, a cottage in the village of Lochearnhead in the Highlands of Scotland and a house in Selston, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works as a roadside mechanic, finding out what it takes to keep the UK's motorists moving. He follows breakdowns from the initial alert, learns techniques for roadside assistance and rescues stranded motorists with one of the largest automotive service companies in the country

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: More stories of near misses, featuring a man, once rejected by the fire service for being too short, rushing into a burning building to save an elderly woman. Plus, a young boxer tries to dodge a car shunted onto the pavement at speed and a driver fails to see a cyclist on a busy dual carriageway. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, West Sussex, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine ready to help the two teams as they vie to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Charlie also visits a family business that specialises in conserving historical artifacts

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: Live coverage of the outcome of yesterday's elections, as results are announced. The Scottish and Welsh national parliaments are up for grabs, as well as mayoral elections in 13 of England's city areas, including London and Manchester. There are also council elections across England and Police and Crime Commissioner votes in England and Wales. Presented by Huw Edwards and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the working week, introducing the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: The latest developments in the day's elections

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise apologises to the Taylors for the hamper but realises her attempts are futile, so she takes further action. Vi introduces herself to Phil and demands he takes her for a drink - but when Kat sees them she wastes no time giving the newcomer a piece of her mind. Bobby's lies catch up with him, while Gray has bad news for Whitney

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Clare Balding, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell and Greg James join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern guest hosts, inviting comedian Zoe Lyons and journalist John Pienaar to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: When Harry Met Sally (1989)
programme.desc: A man and a woman with different attitudes to life meet by chance and develop a close and long-lasting friendship. But over time, a mutual sexual attraction begins to develop and threatens to ruin their relationship - raising the question whether men and women can ever really be just friends. Romantic comedy, starring Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works at a veterinary practice in rural Cumbria, learning why the health of livestock is vital to keeping the country fed, helping to keep working animals in good condition and assisting the vets who provide round-the-clock care

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers is joined by artist Chloe Kempster and blacksmith Kev Paxton as they try to transform an unwanted writing bureau, some car mechanic ramps and a hardwood coffee table

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Exploring Asian hate crime, deaf people doing jury service, still-life painting and the Deaf Mosaic exhibition. With voiceover

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Six budding gardeners and families will make regular appearances throughout the series to share hints from their own gardens all over Scotland to encourage us all to get growing. Mairi and Carole give Beechgrove a good old spring clean. While George is down at his allotment, Kirsty takes a day out at the beach to go searching for seaweed

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the penultimate episode of the quiz in which contestants choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Christine and Jim need a garden overhaul in Urmston, Greater Manchester. In Croydon, Sara wants a new shoe cupboard, with three pairs of builders competing for the jobs

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After what happened with Poppy and Arlo, George and Woody still are not talking, but Hannah has a radical plan to sort them out

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: Steve Backshall visits America's Yellowstone National Park, where bears, coyotes, bison and elk roam vast grasslands, wetlands and forests. He tries to solve the puzzle of why the population of beavers increased when wolves were returned to the area after an absence of 70 years, despite the two species of animal having little to do with each another

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls explores the extraordinary ecosystem that covers an area larger than Great Britain, revealing that only seven per cent of it is coral reef. The rest is a variety of interconnected habitats, including hundreds of islands, mangrove swamps, deep-water gardens, vast sand flats, meadows of sea grass and what is said to be the world's oldest jungle. All are interconnected by a giant deep-water lagoon - and all are full of amazing wildlife

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of the best finds from the show's travels, visiting Lulworth Castle in Dorset, Chiddingstone Castle in Kent, Wrest Park, Bedfordshire and Gloucester Cathedral. Items range from an 'Old Bill' car mascot to a large London-made clock. Plus, Paul Martin finds out more about marquetry, parquetry and inlay at Breamore House in Hampshire

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's travels resume in leafy Hertfordshire, where he attempts a canoe slalom course, and visits the estate of Dame Barbara Cartland

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers ride across the Balearic islands Menorca and Majorca, sampling great produce and finding out about the British influence in the Mediterranean from the 17th century to the present day. They reveal how mayonnaise was invented in Menorca's capital of Mahon, sample Menorcan gin, and meet British chef Mark Fosh, who has lived in Majorca for more than 20 years. Recipes include cuttlefish stew and squid salad with mayonnaise, fish and chips in gin and tonic batter, and sea bass a la mallorquina and saffron potato puree

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Dave Critchley, Dan McGeorge, Ashwani Rangta and Kirk Haworth compete for the North West of England with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. Andi Oliver welcomes them to the kitchen where they each showcase their creations, before one of them leaves the competition

programme.title: The Battle for Britney: Fans, Cash and a Conservatorship
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar attempts to untangle the complexities surrounding the controversial conservatorship Britney Spears has been under for the last 13 years. Mobeen delves into one of the most passionate fan bases in modern celebrity, attending a court hearing along the way and finding himself in the midst of the ongoing battle between Britney's conservatorship team and the passionate fans behind the #FreeBritney movement. The documentary also features never-before-seen interviews with contributors including her former choreographer Brian Friedman and celebrity blogger Perez Hilton

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Guests Colin Lane, Sarah Millican and Noel Fielding join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate the theme 'numbers' in this extended episode. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Detectives: Fighting Organised Crime
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down on a busy residential street in Manchester, a team of specialist GMP detectives race to identify the masked men responsible

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Exploring Asian hate crime, deaf people doing jury service, still-life painting and the Deaf Mosaic exhibition. With voiceover

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Industry titans Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies are entertained by a DJ and dancer pitching a socially distanced nightclub business, while an Irish inventor with a muscle massaging device hopes his proposition will loosen up the Dragons' pockets. A pair of optical consultants have their sights set on investment with their ready-readers business and a 29-year-old tech enthusiast is confident that his clever way of monetising people's personal online data. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Climate Change: Ade on the Frontline
programme.desc: Ade Adepitan visits Bangladesh's Ganges delta, which is under threat from increasingly extreme weather, and joins a government effort to plant huge new areas with mangroves to defend the low-lying country from tidal surges. He then travels to the kingdom of Bhutan to discover how melting Himalayan glaciers affect the entire world, but also hears from the prime minister how his country has become carbon negative

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets his good friend, the actor and comedian Barry Humphries, who fell in love with Cornwall in the 1960s when he escaped London to develop his famous character Dame Edna Everage. Barry talks about his friendship with British poet John Betjeman, and asks Rick to review his home-made fishcakes. Rick also goes fishing for crab, before cooking a simple yet delicious crab omelette. The host then takes a dip in the chilly north Atlantic with an eccentric group of wild water swimmers called the Perranporth Blue Tits

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works with one of the UK's biggest water companies. He traces the flow of this precious resource from the reservoir to homes and businesses, before finding out what happens with wastewater at the other end of the system

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists screen print designer Daniel Heath and clock restorer Paul Parry to rejuvenate an old writing bureau and a 1940s mantle clock. She also tries to cultivate something sellable from fragrant bags of garden waste

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways that some animals use colour to hide and disappear into the background. The Bengal tigers of central India use their striped patterns to dazzle colour blind prey, while zebras on the Masai Mara use motion dazzle to confuse predators. Other animals use colour to trick and to deceive. On Australia's Great Barrier Reef, a blue-striped blenny uses colours to mimic other fish and launch a sudden attack

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Rob Beckett hosts the quiz in which a primary school teacher, a legal writer and a retired telemarketer must choose between working together or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: In Sandhurst, Berkshire, Andy and Ann want their downstairs cloakroom updating, while in Ormskirk, West Lancashire, Naomi and David need extra storage in their kitchen

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: George receives the news that his wife Laura's killer may soon be out on parole, while Shay and Siouxsie pick up a mysterious stranger in their car

programme.title: Nature's Microworlds
programme.desc: The vast majority of life on Earth is under the sea, particularly in deep waters. Steve Backshall turns his attention to the oceans, exploring extraordinary creatures such as vampire squid, angler fish and jellyfish, and reveals how these creatures survive in such a hostile environment

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef
programme.desc: Monty Halls reveals how the Great Barrier Reef is vitally linked to the rest of the planet in many ways. Creatures travel thousands of miles to visit, from tiger sharks, great whales and seabirds to the largest green-turtle gathering on Earth and rarely seen alien-like creatures, such as the tentacled nautilus. The reef is also affected by weather systems travelling from across the Pacific, including cyclones that bring destruction and chaos to the coral and the creatures that live on it

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Gloucester Cathedral, presenter Paul Martin and experts Philip Serrell and Michael Baggott unearth some unusual items among the antiques and collectibles brought along to the valuation day. Philllip discovers there are still bargains to be found at car boot sales, and an exquisite Italian micromosaic necklace sparks Michael's interest. Meanwhile, Paul fulfils a lifetime's dream when he runs away to Gloucester-based circus, Giffords, and learns a few tricks of the trade from its famous clown, Tweedy

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their general knowledge skills ahead of Friday's all-important final

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for the Hertfordshire village of Perry Green to learn about Henry Moore became one of the defining artists of British modernism. In Cambridge, he revisits his former university to hear about a treacherous time in its past

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. At home, he cooks a memorable dish of steak frites with Bordelaise sauce

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs compete with dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. One pins their hopes on a dish celebrating a church minister who was a pioneer of vegetarianism, while another is inspired by a Victorian female adventurer. The third chef presents a dish with a nod to the first public wash house. But whose mouthwatering plate will please tough veteran judge Tom Aikens the most? Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Marc, a public health worker who was diagnosed with HIV aged 17 in the late 1980s. He found support at the Landmark Centre in Brixton via a volunteer called John. Nearly 30 years later, Marc is desperate to thank John for helping him. Plus, Belfast dad Peter searches for a rehabilitation nurse called Betsy who helped him recover from a gunshot wound during the Troubles

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Maff Brown, Ria Lina, Ed Byrne and Rhys James. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After advertising her home on Airbnb, Mandy takes in a guest from Russia - and they soon become more than friends. But can he be trusted? Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Tony Way (After Life)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Mark Urban and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Up in the Air (2009)
programme.desc: A corporate downsizing expert loves his life, detached from everyday existence and striving to achieve a personal goal of 10 million accumulated air miles. However, his lifestyle comes under threat by an up-and-coming executive with a revolutionary approach that would make his role all but obsolete. Comedy drama, starring George Clooney, Vera Farmiga, Anna Kendrick, Jason Bateman and Amy Morton

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Taking place in a specially built workshop nestled in Birmingham's historic Jewellery Quarter, Katherine Ryan hosts as talented jewellers battle it out to prove they can be the next big jewellery-making sensation. This time, the seven remaining contestants are challenged to make a chain collar and a sweetheart brooch, before one of them leaves the competition

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It's summer week in the sewing room as the contestants are challenged to create three garments too look and feel good in the heat. They begin by following the pattern for a pair of paperbag shorts, before transforming second-hand men's swimming gear into a women's cocktail outfit. The made-to-measure challenge sees them taking on button-down sundresses, and eagle-eyed judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly aligned buttonholes and secure fastenings. Who will miss the mark and be second to leave? Joe Lycett has the answer

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick journeys inland from the majestic Camel Estuary to one of Britain's finest vineyards, where he joins in harvesting the grapes to discover that the Cornish weather is perfect making some of the best wine in the country. Just off Pentire Point, as the sun sets, Rick rides the gentle swell of the north Atlantic with an old friend, casting his line to hook Britain's fastest fish, before firing up the barbeque to cook his catch with a recipe inspired from a trip to Goa. Rick joins Tim Smit, best known for starting the hugely successful Eden Project, who reveals to Rick his new and ambitious idea in Cornwall

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works in construction, finding out what it takes to join the men and women who are building Britain. Around 2.4 million people work in the construction industry, making it one of the largest employers in the UK - worth over £100billion pounds in 2019 alone

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph goes on the lookout for quality discards at Walsall Recycling Centre, in the West Midlands. There are pine kitchen units to be transformed with the help of carpenter Bruce Faulseit and some life jackets and water skiing ropes that bag-maker Rosanna Gethin hopes to make shipshape. It's a pair of mid-century dining chairs that Jacqui herself sets to work on

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the experts get to work on more heirlooms to bring them, and the memories they hold, back to life. Dominic Chinea faces the heavy-duty task of repairing a seized-up retro roundabout that reminds its owner Stella of her three daughters' childhoods. Wood expert Will Kirk is amazed when a First World War dominoes set, thought to have been played in the Christmas truce of 1914, arrives in the barn. Suzie Fletcher uses her leather skills on a 19th-century women's side saddle bought in Texas, and Julie and Amanda need the help of mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher to fix a broken teddy bear

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: Huw Edwards and Kirsty Wark are your guides as the votes are counted in this year's bumper set of elections. Correspondents will be at all the key counts to bring the latest results from the Scottish Parliament and Welsh Senedd elections - and from all the key contests for local councils, mayors and police commissioners across England

programme.title: Inside the Perfect Predator
programme.desc: Documentary using ground-breaking computer graphics and close-up photography to examine four of the natural world's most extraordinary hunters. The programme follows a peregrine falcon on a mission to kill for its chicks, a great white shark hungry for seal blubber, a cheetah struggling to provide for its cubs and a Nile crocodile poised to ambush its dinner

programme.title: Britain's Most Spectacular Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Piers Taylor challenge three backyard inventors to let their imaginations run wild and come up with unique and visually stunning designs, including a sweet-dispensing carousel, an automated breakfast maker and a giant robot

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives for spicy and exotic dishes, including tapas by James Martin, Paul Hollywood's tortilla tower, a Jamaican ginger cake from the Hairy Bikers and Ainsley's own jerk pork

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah meets two expats relocating to the New Forest after 20 years in America

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore puts in some hard graft at the nation's tips to uncover four items that can be transformed. Master carpenter Norman Wilkinson, upholsterer Anthony Devine and design guru Jay Blades are on hand to help reinvent the pieces of furniture into desirable pieces. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that saw action in the First World War, a silver Welsh crown of great cultural significance, and a quirky tortoise-shaped bell

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Suffolk on the last leg of his 1930s Bradshaw's inspired tour of East Anglia, visiting the racecourse at Newmarket and the Roman Catholic shrine at Walsingham

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik, coastal capital of Iceland. His aim is to enjoy the perfect winter break, enriched with a smorgasbord featuring the best ingredients the country has to offer - fjord-reared roast lamb, rye bread baked in volcanic sand and langoustine soup. Rick also resumes his search for the perfect cod

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the North West go head-to-head cooking their entire six-course menus for the judges, including Wayne Hemingway MBE. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In Adam Frost's garden, he gives his tips on lawn repair, grows some unusual vegetables and plants a seasonal container. Meanwhile, Advolly Richmond brings us up to date in her history of lawns

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Johnny Vegas, Sue Perkins, Mike Tindall, John Barnes and Fleur East play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: End of Watch (2012)
programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. The officers end up in over their heads when they stumble on evidence that could bring down a drug cartel, putting them squarely in the gang's firing line. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: President Roslin's leadership is challenged when the crew of Galactica join forces with Cylon rebels to bring down the Resurrection Hub. In a state of turmoil, she boards the Hybrid's ship, which then flees the fleet, taking the leader away from her people to an unknown location. Mary McDonnell stars

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The colonials are left shocked in the wake of President Roslin's abduction, and a bitter power struggle erupts, threatening to tear the fleet apart. Mary McDonnell and Edward James Olmos star

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Jemma Woodman goes on a culinary tour of the river Dart, meeting those who get inspiration and food from this scenic part of the South West.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas a picturesque spot in the meadow, with a couple of serene fishing holes

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts shares the best archaeological discoveries of 2018, starting with the north of the UK and a preserved prehistoric village at the black Loch in Myrton. The programme also follows archaeologists uncovering a previously unknown Anglo-Saxon cemetery in Lincolnshire and a major regeneration project in Salford, unearthing the largest jail in Georgian England

programme.title: The 1951 Festival of Britain: Brave New World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the 1951 Festival of Britain helped the nation to look forward to a brighter future during a period of post-war austerity. Archive footage and personal testimonies reveal how, despite widespread debt and rationing, the event encouraged people to have fun through design and ingenuity

programme.title: The Treasure Hunters
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell and Ellie Harrison present the first of two programmes in which they embark on the ultimate treasure hunt, searching the globe to uncover its extraordinary riches, from natural gems to precious metals - and more besides. Dallas begins his search Down Under, free-diving for lustrous pearls in the waters around north-west Australia and seeking out diamonds from the bottom of the ocean. He also learns about opal mining in one of the most hostile places on Earth and, together with Ellie, reveals how ambergris, a substance that starts life in a sperm whale's stomach, can prove an expensive ingredient in perfume. Plus, how one of the largest and most unusual treasures has helped solve a 67-million-year-old puzzle

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed over five days, this programme examines the complex and time-consuming process involved in building a classic Windsor chair, made by craftsman Jim Steele in his Warwickshire workshop. The traditional design features woods chosen for their different qualities - ash, elm and yew - and the finished object has just two screws and no glue

programme.title: Bacchus Uncovered: Ancient God of Ecstasy
programme.desc: Professor Bettany Hughes investigates the story of Bacchus, travelling to Georgia, Britain, Jordan and Greece to discover his origins and his presence in the modern world

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross has more painting tips as he captures on canvas a picturesque spot in the meadow, with a couple of serene fishing holes

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Jemma Woodman goes on a culinary tour of the river Dart, meeting those who get inspiration and food from this scenic part of the South West.

programme.title: The 1951 Festival of Britain: Brave New World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the 1951 Festival of Britain helped the nation to look forward to a brighter future during a period of post-war austerity. Archive footage and personal testimonies reveal how, despite widespread debt and rationing, the event encouraged people to have fun through design and ingenuity

programme.title: The Violence Paradox
programme.desc: In the second of the two-part documentary, psychologist Steven Pinker examines evidence that suggests the world is becoming more peaceful, including how income equality and personal contact through sport may curb violence in modern societies

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher walks an eight-mile route along the River Lea, taking in the landmarks of an industrial past and discovering what the future holds for the waterway in this fast-growing part of the capital

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross reveals how to capture on canvas the beauty of a still winter's day, with a happy sun shining bright in the sky

programme.title: Citizen Kane (1941)
programme.desc: After the death of an influential newspaper magnate, an inquisitive reporter begins piecing together the details of his life, discovering an epic tale of ambition and conflict - all the while trying to discover the meaning behind the millionaire's enigmatic last word. Oscar-winning drama, written, directed by and starring Orson Welles. With Joseph Cotten, Dorothy Comingore and William Alland

programme.title: The Eyes of Orson Welles (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary presenting a portrait of the director, with access to an archive of his artwork and writing, providing an insight into incomplete projects and personal inspirations. The film is divided into four sections, exploring Welles' political beliefs, his romantic life, his fascination with power and corruption, and his sense of humour

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed in an industrial forge lit only by the welding torch and burning metal, this programme examines the process involved in the crafting of a steel knife. Bladesmith Owen Bush uses a variety of techniques, both ancient and modern, and takes his time over the complex skill of shaping and blending the steel into a knife

programme.title: A History of Art in Three Colours
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox concludes his examination of the three colours he considers most important to human history by arguing that white, traditionally a symbol of virtue and purity, was warped into a shade that exposed the darkest human instincts. He explores why the colour fascinated 18th-century potter Josiah Wedgwood and art historian Johann Joachim Winckelmann, and examines how in the 20th century it came to be linked with the Italian dictator Mussolini and fascism

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross reveals how to capture on canvas the beauty of a still winter's day, with a happy sun shining bright in the sky

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher walks an eight-mile route along the River Lea, taking in the landmarks of an industrial past and discovering what the future holds for the waterway in this fast-growing part of the capital

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: Mark looks at some of the best films about British history in the order of when they are set, tracing the story of Britain from the Roman invasion to the modern era, via medieval times, the Tudors, the Civil War and the 18th and 19th centuries

programme.title: Easy Listening Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by some of the best-known easy-listening musicians of the 1960s and '70s, including Sergio Mendes & Brasil 66, Burt Bacharach, the Carpenters and Andy Williams

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on December 13. With performances by Shakin' Stevens, Malandra Burrows, Seal, Chris Isaak, Dimples D, the Righteous Brothers, Yazoo, Black Box and Vanilla Ice

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on December 20. With performances by Snap, Cliff Richard, Vanilla Ice, the Carpenters, INXS, Enigma, MC Hammer, Status Quo, Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers, and John Travolta and Olivia Newton-John

programme.title: Emmylou Harris: From a Deeper Well
programme.desc: Profile of the highly respected singer, who has spent the past 30 years building an impressive body of country music. Discovered by Gram Parsons, Harris and her former backing group the Hot Band revolutionised the genre in the 1970s and 1980s, re-interpreting it for successive generations of fans. She then went on to release Wrecking Ball, one of the most critically acclaimed albums of the 1990s. Featuring interviews with friends, colleagues and admirers including Elvis Costello, Willie Nelson and Keith Richards

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: A performance from Los Angeles by the American musician, highlighting the rules she believes make a great country song. Each track illustrates one of her 10 commandments, with a short introduction to explain why it was chosen and what element of the genre it best represents

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: A one-off concert by country star Emmylou Harris and the Hot Band recorded for the Old Grey Whistle Test in 1977, including performances of Luxury Liner and Tulsa Queen

programme.title: Sisters in Country: Dolly, Linda and Emmylou
programme.desc: How Dolly Parton, Linda Ronstadt and Emmylou Harris's careers took off in the 1970s with very distinct takes on country music, before they came together to collaborate on a successful album in 1987. The artists talk about uniting as harmony singers, focusing on how their alliance made them pioneers in bringing different music worlds together and raising the game for women in the country tradition. Narrated by KT Tunstall

programme.title: Country at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of BBC session performances by country artists from the past four decades, including Tammy Wynette, Emmylou Harris, Johnny Cash, Dolly Parton, Charley Pride, Kris Kristofferson, Garth Brooks, Willie Nelson, Alison Krauss, Taylor Swift, kd lang and Billie Jo Spears

programme.title: Mimi and Co
programme.desc: It’s Halloween and Mimi is on a mission to prank her best friend, Ashleigh, who claims she is not afraid of anything. She has found a spooky cave rumoured to be haunted by a ghost searching for his own heart, which was stolen several hundred years ago. Mimi also introduces sisters Angel, Melody and Nevaeh, who are super-proud of their traveller heritage and go trick or treating on their plot of land. Another Halloween fan is TikTok phenomenon Fats Timbo, who is Mimi’s Q&A guest and tells us how she has managed to use her platform to empower herself and others. Ashleigh is unimpressed when she finally arrives at the cave - until a surprise visitor gives her the fright of her life.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. Can Joe fully recover from his surgery and complete the training that will allow him to have a new best friend - his very own hearing dog?

Matilda's in charge of the party food: build your own spicy sausage pitta bread sandwiches with zingy yoghurt sauce and popcorn cakes. programme.title: Would You Rather
programme.desc: Would You Rather? is a brand new series for CBBC hosted by comedian Darren Harriott. In this episode, Darren is joined by social media sensations Adam Beales and Joe Tasker, alongside his usual crew of 15 kids, as they face some big and bamboozling dilemmas to debate. Our celebrity mates Adam and Joe are tasked with trying to think just like Darren’s Crew and guess which way they vote on a series of hilarious Would You Rather? dilemmas. This episode features fun dilemmas, such as Would You Rather… Have ears the size of an elephant or a trunk instead of a nose? Have hair that changes colour depending on the temperature or have eyes that change colour depending on your mood? Time Travel into the past or time travel into the future? Tune in to hear Adam and Joe’s hilarious answers, as well as the Crew’s amusing insights.

All the parents and babies have books with different words and pictures too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to hide a baby-appropriate book inside a fabric bag or pillowcase to explore with their baby. Some viewers at home show how they’ve been reading books and sharing stories too. The nursery rhyme This Is the Way We Read a Book features, as does a story about Baby Bear’s favourite book. programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome Zuri, Otis, Theo, Reau, Elijah and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Giovanna has a tambourine inside her bag. All the parents and babies have different noisy objects and toys in their bags too, which are explored through play, songs and a story. Parents, carers, and their little ones at home are encouraged to hide baby-appropriate noisy toys and objects inside a fabric bag or pillowcase, and then play with the items together! The fun children’s song Noisy Noisy Shaker features, as does a story about Baby Bear and Baby Tiger making lots of noise, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring sounds too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Kiri & Lou

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Red Squirrels Flint and Hazel really don’t like tidying, so their corner of the woods is a mess. But today Auntie the Tawny Owl's coming to visit, so they’d better clear up. When the rain and wind starts blowing branches down, they’ll never be ready on time. What are they going to do?

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers go on a special autumn walk that starts in the daytime and finishes in the dark, where they all get to drink hot chocolate. On the way, they discover where a beaver has been at work, play inside a tree, make shadows with their lanterns and even fly like bats!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: You Are Old, Father William by Lewis Carroll Wynken, Blynken and Nod by Eugene Field Look at what my hands can do! In this two poem episode, Aimee and Ashley are visited by a silvery, slithery eel and lots of twinkling herring fish. Ashley meets Bobbles who takes the role of old Father William to do some impressive back-flips and balance an eel on the end of his nose. Aimee also sails the skies in a wooden shoe with Leapy the Lion, Dixie the Dog and Click Clack the Crab as magical men who are fishing for stars

programme.title: Patchwork Pals

programme.title: Tik Tak

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Charlie, Etti and their families as they discover the world around them at three months old and learn what makes them both smile. We join Etti, who is having a fun day out with her family at some local gardens. They see lots of different flowers and animals including a big rabbit statue! But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of flowers to see how we can help to look after them and our planet. Find out how flowers and different animals, like bees, birds, butterflies and insects, work together. We learn how bees and insects pollinate flowers, as Maddie becomes a bee and plays the pollination game! Along the way, we’ll discover flowers of all different shapes, sizes and colours and learn why they are so different. We discover the different parts of a flower, the stigma, the anther, the stamen and the petals and how some flowers go to sleep at night and open in the day!

Plus, a revisit to a church in Dover that Martin Roberts first saw five years ago

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works with one of the UK's biggest water companies. He traces the flow of this precious resource from the reservoir to homes and businesses, before finding out what happens with wastewater at the other end of the system

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists screen print designer Daniel Heath and clock restorer Paul Parry to rejuvenate an old writing bureau and a 1940s mantle clock. She also tries to cultivate something sellable from fragrant bags of garden waste

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park code breaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered old Victorian firefighter's helmet. A threadbare teddy on wheels is also restored

programme.title: Pointless
