programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway visits south Wales and discovers a walk of contrasts that includes a beach, a canal and a bustling marina. On the way he learns about Welsh tarantulas and narrowly avoids crashing a luxury boat, while wildlife expert Lizzie Daly goes on a bat hunt and party planner Onkar Singh Purewal organises a tea-party in a ghost village

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. Kirsten Ramsay is called upon to painstakingly piece together a vintage ceramic night light, fractured into dozens of fragments, and skateboard enthusiast Mike is hoping Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea can restore his wooden board, which transports him back to his youth in 1960s Liverpool. Finally, toy restorers Julie and Amanda undertake the repair of a treasured teddy bear that has been by the side of a young man as he's undergone life-changing surgery

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants go global, taking on garments and techniques from around the world. For the pattern challenge they make a French breton top - and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young will be looking for perfectly matched stripes and a completely flat neck - before they transform sarongs into completely different garments. In the final round, the sewers must come up with an outfit inspired by Mexican artist Frida Kahlo, known as much for her personal style as for her vivid artwork. Presented by Joe Lycett

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Greg McKenzie meets young people born or brought up in the UK who say Home Office immigration policy treats them as second class citizens. They cannot vote, cannot claim benefits, struggle to go to university and may even face deportation. Panorama investigates Home Office policy which gives them a chance to stay in the UK, but which takes 10 years and costs thousands of pounds

programme.title: Unsolved: An Alibi for Omar?
programme.desc: Journalist Bronagh Munro uncovers new evidence casting doubt on the conviction of a man who has spent 19 years in prison for murder. Omar Benguit was jailed for killing 26-year-old Korean student Jong-Ok Shin in Bournemouth in 2002. Now, Bronagh tracks down some of the alleged witnesses to ask them what they remember. She also finds evidence that could give Omar Benguit an alibi for the night of the murder

programme.title: Boxing: Canelo Alvarez
programme.desc: A profile of the Mexican boxer, who has won world titles in four different weight classes

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Fleur East, as they assist two friends from London in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing acrobat, a princess and a karate champion

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville meet the devoted dog owners who insured their precious pets only to find out it didn't pay out when disaster struck. Julia also hears from people looking for love who found themselves shelling out more than they expected with one online dating site

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a property in Southampton in a stunning setting, right beside the river, and another in Wellington, Shropshire being renovated with family very much in mind. Plus, a revisit to Clay Cross, Derbyshire, where in October 2018 Dion Dublin saw a plot of land with exciting development potential

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works in construction, finding out what it takes to join the men and women who are building Britain. Around 2.4 million people work in the construction industry, making it one of the largest employers in the UK - worth over £100billion pounds in 2019 alone

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: More stories of near misses, featuring a teenage girl crashing into a bridge during a go-kart race, while her anxious parents watch in the stands. Plus, a kayaking mother and daughter stranded on mud flats after getting caught out by the tide. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe guide two teams scouring Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire for bargains to take to auction. The reds show David how to be cool, while the blues go in search of a duck-billed platypus. Plus, presenter Eric Knowles pays a visit to the Museum of Timekeeping in nearby Upton to find out about a local man's mission to save the St Pancras Railway Clock

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie continues on her quest, but a surprise awaits as she follows her next lead. Tensions run high as the Mill Team faces formidable competition on the sports pitch

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Isle of Arran to house-hunt with a novelist and her husband who are in search of writing inspiration from the Scottish wilderness for their £375,000 budget. Along the way, Jules discovers the positive benefits to Arran's marine habitats and the local fishing industry since Scotland's first 'No Take Zone' was introduced

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph goes on the lookout for quality discards at Walsall Recycling Centre, in the West Midlands. There are pine kitchen units to be transformed with the help of carpenter Bruce Faulseit and some life jackets and water skiing ropes that bag-maker Rosanna Gethin hopes to make shipshape. Jacqui herself sets to work on a pair of mid-century dining chairs

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea welcomes 93-year-old Dora Paul to the workshop, along with the unusual item she's brought in for repair - an old tram seat that once ferried passengers on the number 81 tram in Salford. A vintage horse-racing game and two stained-glass panels also receive attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones co-hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen is asked to record a TV appeal for Bailey but her fears get the better of her - and to make matters worse, nasty comments on the fundraising page threaten to send her over the edge. Phil insists that Callum fix things between him and Ben, Mick has a decision to make, and Dotty disapproves of Sonia's gesture

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Self-made millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies observe a pitch by a plucky entrepreneur with lofty ambitions for his genetically personalised meal shakes. A married couple hope their alcoholic gift box business will make for an intoxicating proposition and the Dragons' are put to the test with a virtual escape room business - will they unlock their coffers and strike a deal? Finally, a 'head nit-picker' is itching to show off her headlice treatment and clinic. Whether the Dragons share her passion for parasites remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the homeowners whose garden sheds have been left facing the axe, due to their local council's planning regulations. Julia also meets a woman whose act of charity was scuppered when her delivery company lost the parcels she was sending to help others

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a two-bedroom flat in Grays in Essex, a cottage in the village of Lochearnhead in the Highlands of Scotland and a house in Selston, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works as a roadside mechanic, finding out what it takes to keep the UK's motorists moving. He follows breakdowns from the initial alert, learns techniques for roadside assistance and rescues stranded motorists with one of the largest automotive service companies in the country

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: More stories of near misses, featuring a man, once rejected by the fire service for being too short, rushing into a burning building to save an elderly woman. Plus, a young boxer tries to dodge a car shunted onto the pavement at speed and a driver fails to see a cyclist on a busy dual carriageway. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, West Sussex, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine ready to help the two teams as they vie to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Charlie also visits a family business that specialises in conserving historical artifacts

programme.title: Wales Today Election Results 2021
programme.desc: Nick Servini, Felicity Evans and Jennifer Jones present full coverage of the 2021 Senedd election results. The Senedd is based in Cardiff and is a distinct entity from the House of Commons in Westminster, which makes laws covering the whole of the UK. The Senedd - known as the National Assembly for Wales until May 2020 - runs health, education, local government, transport, housing, economic development, social services, culture, Welsh language policy, agriculture and other public services. There are 60 members of the Senedd, 40 of whom represent individual constituencies while 20 represent the five regions of Wales

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: In March 2020, university student Ellis Lloyd Jones, locked-down and bored, began turning out more and more of the TikTok videos he'd started posting five months earlier. Soon his posts began to go viral. Here, he reveals the truth behind his success. Laura Mallows reveals the meteoric and unusual rise of her vegan beauty products company Mallows Beauty. Last spring, the pandemic came as a big blow to her fledgling business, but with presence of mind Laura came up with a way out of her predicament as she became one of the first people in the UK to apply for a licence to manufacture hand-sanitizer

programme.title: When Harry Met Sally (1989)
programme.desc: A man and a woman with different attitudes to life meet by chance and develop a close and long-lasting friendship. But over time, a mutual sexual attraction begins to develop and threatens to ruin their relationship - raising the question whether men and women can ever really be just friends. Romantic comedy, starring Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Amber learns the secret Alex has been hiding, but doesn't know how to deal with it, while Stevie makes an impulsive decision that has consequences. Kim ponders her relationship, and Cameron sinks his claws deeper into Dylan

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher works on a mantle clock in the shape of a ship, a treasured gift from the owner's late grandfather. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise apologises to the Taylors for the hamper but realises her attempts are futile, so she takes further action. Vi introduces herself to Phil and demands he takes her for a drink - but when Kat sees them she wastes no time giving the newcomer a piece of her mind. Bobby's lies catch up with him, while Gray has bad news for Whitney

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Kelso, to celebrate the 250th anniversary of literary great and Borders boy Walter Scott and explore the landscape that inspired him. Arlene Stuart meets a modern day farmer and author Patrick Laurie, who is encouraging other rural writers to 'get stuck in', while Euan McIlwraith points the focus on one of his favourite birds, the eider duck. Plus, the first of a new series of features about farms cutting their environmental impact sees Anne Lundon pay a visit to a dairy farm that has ditched soya in favour of a home-grown feed

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern guest hosts, inviting comedian Zoe Lyons and journalist John Pienaar to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

Panorama investigates Home Office policy which gives them a chance to stay in the UK, but which takes 10 years and costs thousands of pounds

programme.title: Unsolved: An Alibi for Omar?
programme.desc: Journalist Bronagh Munro uncovers new evidence casting doubt on the conviction of a man who has spent 19 years in prison for murder. Omar Benguit was jailed for killing 26-year-old Korean student Jong-Ok Shin in Bournemouth in 2002. Now, Bronagh tracks down some of the alleged witnesses to ask them what they remember. She also finds evidence that could give Omar Benguit an alibi for the night of the murder

programme.title: Boxing: Canelo Alvarez
programme.desc: A profile of the Mexican boxer, who has won world titles in four different weight classes

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville meet the devoted dog owners who insured their precious pets only to find out it didn't pay out when disaster struck. Julia also hears from people looking for love who found themselves shelling out more than they expected with one online dating site

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a property in Southampton in a stunning setting, right beside the river, and another in Wellington, Shropshire being renovated with family very much in mind. Plus, a revisit to Clay Cross, Derbyshire, where in October 2018 Dion Dublin saw a plot of land with exciting development potential

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works in construction, finding out what it takes to join the men and women who are building Britain. Around 2.4 million people work in the construction industry, making it one of the largest employers in the UK - worth over £100billion pounds in 2019 alone

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: More stories of near misses, featuring a teenage girl crashing into a bridge during a go-kart race, while her anxious parents watch in the stands. Plus, a kayaking mother and daughter stranded on mud flats after getting caught out by the tide. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe guide two teams scouring Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire for bargains to take to auction. The reds show David how to be cool, while the blues go in search of a duck-billed platypus. Plus, presenter Eric Knowles pays a visit to the Museum of Timekeeping in nearby Upton to find out about a local man's mission to save the St Pancras Railway Clock

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie continues on her quest, but a surprise awaits as she follows her next lead. Tensions run high as the Mill Team faces formidable competition on the sports pitch

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to the Isle of Arran to house-hunt with a novelist and her husband who are in search of writing inspiration from the Scottish wilderness for their £375,000 budget. Along the way, Jules discovers the positive benefits to Arran's marine habitats and the local fishing industry since Scotland's first 'No Take Zone' was introduced

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph goes on the lookout for quality discards at Walsall Recycling Centre, in the West Midlands. There are pine kitchen units to be transformed with the help of carpenter Bruce Faulseit and some life jackets and water skiing ropes that bag-maker Rosanna Gethin hopes to make shipshape. Jacqui herself sets to work on a pair of mid-century dining chairs

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metalworker Dominic Chinea welcomes 93-year-old Dora Paul to the workshop, along with the unusual item she's brought in for repair - an old tram seat that once ferried passengers on the number 81 tram in Salford. A vintage horse-racing game and two stained-glass panels also receive attention

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones co-hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen is asked to record a TV appeal for Bailey but her fears get the better of her - and to make matters worse, nasty comments on the fundraising page threaten to send her over the edge. Phil insists that Callum fix things between him and Ben, Mick has a decision to make, and Dotty disapproves of Sonia's gesture

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Self-made millionaires Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Sara Davies observe a pitch by a plucky entrepreneur with lofty ambitions for his genetically personalised meal shakes. A married couple hope their alcoholic gift box business will make for an intoxicating proposition and the Dragons' are put to the test with a virtual escape room business - will they unlock their coffers and strike a deal? Finally, a 'head nit-picker' is itching to show off her headlice treatment and clinic. Whether the Dragons share her passion for parasites remains to be seen. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers reviews the week's political news, inviting comment and analysis from Stormont and Westminster

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the homeowners whose garden sheds have been left facing the axe, due to their local council's planning regulations. Julia also meets a woman whose act of charity was scuppered when her delivery company lost the parcels she was sending to help others

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a two-bedroom flat in Grays in Essex, a cottage in the village of Lochearnhead in the Highlands of Scotland and a house in Selston, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works as a roadside mechanic, finding out what it takes to keep the UK's motorists moving. He follows breakdowns from the initial alert, learns techniques for roadside assistance and rescues stranded motorists with one of the largest automotive service companies in the country

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: More stories of near misses, featuring a man, once rejected by the fire service for being too short, rushing into a burning building to save an elderly woman. Plus, a young boxer tries to dodge a car shunted onto the pavement at speed and a driver fails to see a cyclist on a busy dual carriageway. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, West Sussex, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine ready to help the two teams as they vie to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Charlie also visits a family business that specialises in conserving historical artifacts

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: Live coverage of the outcome of yesterday's elections, as results are announced. The Scottish and Welsh national parliaments are up for grabs, as well as mayoral elections in 13 of England's city areas, including London and Manchester. There are also council elections across England and Police and Crime Commissioner votes in England and Wales. Presented by Huw Edwards and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the working week, introducing the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: The latest developments in the day's elections

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise apologises to the Taylors for the hamper but realises her attempts are futile, so she takes further action. Vi introduces herself to Phil and demands he takes her for a drink - but when Kat sees them she wastes no time giving the newcomer a piece of her mind. Bobby's lies catch up with him, while Gray has bad news for Whitney

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Clare Balding, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell and Greg James join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern guest hosts, inviting comedian Zoe Lyons and journalist John Pienaar to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Highlights edition of the hit comedy panel show. Regulars Tim McGarry, Neil Delamere and Colin Murphy are joined by a selection of guests from the recent series, including Diona Doherty, Laurie Kilmartin, Jimeoin and Paul Sinha

programme.title: When Harry Met Sally (1989)
programme.desc: A man and a woman with different attitudes to life meet by chance and develop a close and long-lasting friendship. But over time, a mutual sexual attraction begins to develop and threatens to ruin their relationship - raising the question whether men and women can ever really be just friends. Romantic comedy, starring Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: The morning's business in the Northern Ireland Assembly including the consideration of the Pension Schemes Bill and a series of motions on coronavirus regulations covering Northern Ireland

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: The afternoon's business in the Northern Ireland Assembly including questions to the Executive Office covering the First Minister and the deputy First Minister, environment questions and the motion on regulations covering corporate insolvency

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport Committee session on concussion in sport with evidence from Monica Petrosino, TeamGB ice hockey player, and Eleanor Furneaux, TeamGB skeleton bobsleigher, as well as the Geoff Astle Foundation, Progressive Rugby, the Football Association and the Rugby Football Union, from Tuesday 23 March.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport Committee's session on concussion in sport with evidence from the Rugby Players Association, the Professional Players Federation, the Professional Footballers Association and health experts, from Tuesday 27 April

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Environmental Audit select committee session on fixing fashion with evidence from Primark, Refashion, ReLondon, the Waste and Resources Action Programme and Renewal Workshop, from Wednesday 28 April.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The House of Lords select committee on the national plan for sport and recreation looking at the barriers to sport and recreation for people from ethnic minority communities, and women and girls, with evidence from Kick It Out, Sporting Equals, Black Girls Hike, Women in Sport, the Football Association and the Muslim Sport Foundation, from Wednesday 21 April.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Digital, Culture, Media and Sport select committee session on sport in our communities with evidence from the Sport for Development Coalition, the Sport and Recreation Alliance, UK Athletics, Swim England and the GB Wheelchair Rugby, from Tuesday 13 May.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: The debate in Westminster Hall, the second Commons chamber, on the promotion of electric vehicle usage, from Wednesday 21st April.

programme.title: Westminster Hall
programme.desc: The debate in Westminster Hall on the UK's accession to the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership, from Wednesday 21st April.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: Talking Movies reports on the excitement at Union Station in Los Angeles, which is co-hosting this year's Academy Awards. Several big name stars tell the programme team how they view the 2021 Oscars contest, which has the most diverse field of contenders ever. British actor Riz Ahmed discusses the significance of becoming the first Muslim to be nominated for a best actor Oscar. Also, celebrated Danish film-maker Thomas Vinterberg discusses his drinking drama Another Round, favoured to win for best international feature film. The programme will also reveal what millions of viewers around the world can expect to see when they tune in to watch this in-person Oscars ceremony, which has been significantly reinvented because of the global pandemic.

programme.title: Sgeulachd Deacon Brodie
programme.desc: John Morrison explores the life of Edinburgh-born William Brodie, whose criminal dealings and double identity is believed to have inspired Robert Louis Stevenson's character Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: The princess races through the woods and comes face to face with a wolf

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Pàdraig has to deliver an old steam engine from Cornwall for Seonaidh's cousin, Ronaidh

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shanes want to find hazelnuts for his dish of the day so he, Izzy and Mario head to the woods to forage for some

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: Seren is chosen to go in search of cherries

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: Rita and Crocodile are playing croquet in the garden, but Crocodile is much better at playing than Rita

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Angela Mackay reads Zoe agus Beans: Càite Bheil Binky Boo? by Chloe Inkpen, illustrated by Mick Inkpen and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Danger Mouse, Penfold, Squawk and Colonel K must team up with accidental duplicates of themselves to stop Big Head

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Ronia and Biorc think that there is nowhere as delightful as the forest. They would like to tame some horses so that they can go riding – and this will require dedication

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Matilda returns to Paper Port to spend her summer vacation. While she was at home, she had no powers and she believes it's Paper Port that directly activates them

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Mairi MacRitchie enlists the help of three skilled cooks to prepare a festive feast, including mince pies, a marzipan-filled stollen and a rich Christmas cake for pudding. Plus, recipes for cocktails and canapes will be shared, and ideas for edible gifts and decorations

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Dan
programme.desc: Linda MacLeod reads the poem “Mar Chuimhneachan air Mo Mhac Ghràdhach” which was written by her great aunt, Morag MacDonald, after losing her son

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Leugh Mi (Book Show)
programme.desc: Anna Macleod is joined in conversation by Skye Gaelic singer Eilidh Cormack and TV researcher Euan MacLean from Glasgow, who discuss books of special interest to them

programme.title: Peter Manuel - Deireadh an Uilc
programme.desc: Docu-drama about the serial killer, raising questions about his mental state in relation to his death penalty sentence. Presented by John Morrison, and starring Andrew Clark

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Uisdean prepares a colourful crab starter using brown and velvet crab meat, while Ruaridh makes an interesting crab linguini and creates hot and cold crab spring rolls

programme.title: Bana-Churaidhean/Warrior Women
programme.desc: The story of female Viking warriors, including a woman's quest to avenge her chieftain father's death, which led to her becoming the leader of an important settlement

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean tackles the Firth of Forth, swimming the mile and a half that connects Edinburgh to Fife

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon heads to Argyll's Slate Islands. Known as the 'Islands that Roofed the World', he learns about the rich history of the slate quarrying industry

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Treubh an Tuathanais : Seann Chleasan is Cù Ùr
programme.desc: Cailean wants to be helpful today but things aren't free of complications

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: The shrew crew is planning a feast, so Mossy sets out to collect berries. Oona and Baba help their friend, but find they are left with just a single berry at the end of the day

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Pàdraig has to deliver an old steam engine from Cornwall for Seonaidh's cousin, Ronaidh

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: A trial takes place in the garden with Papa Mùman overseeing matters

programme.title: Treud na Dlùth-choille - Grad-Naidheachd (Jungle Bunch)
programme.desc: The jungle inhabitants are unhappy that the spiders are taking over their patch

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Eilidh Cormack reads An Crogall Nach Robh Measail air Uisge written and illustrated by Gemma Merino and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: In the shallows of Hawaii, life is hard. Catching enough food and avoiding bigger sharks isn't easy and only one in 10 baby hammerheads survives to see its first birthday. If she reaches the age of one Hula will have to leave Hawaii, otherwise she will starve. This means an epic journey to find waters teeming with life, thousands of kilometres away. Here, cameras observe as she learns ocean survival skills

programme.title: An Rud As Fheàrr Leam
programme.desc: Finlay from Ross-shire talks about his favourite hobbies, which include river swimming, driving a tractor and cycling

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: The gang are busy, but the day ends with disappointment

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: A storm brings about much excitement

programme.title: An Taghadh
programme.desc: The BBC Naidheachdan team is live from Inverness with an hour-long special on the first day of results from the Scottish Parliamentary Election 2021

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod visits the Outer Hebrides, where he climbs Harris's highest hill and visits the desolate island of Scarp as he concludes his investigation into the history of place names in the Highlands

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather Dewar travels to Gairloch, where she meets local radio presenter and former teacher Alice Mackenzie, and draws a colour portrait of her

programme.title: A' Fagail Mhiughlaidh
programme.desc: Documentary about the legacy of the deserted Isle of Mingulay, as told through its history, story and song

programme.title: Eadar na Dha: 1918-1935/Clash of Futures: 1918-1935
programme.desc: In order to meet its obligations, Germany fires up the printing presses to produce more money, but hyperinflation ensues

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean tackles the Firth of Forth, swimming the mile and a half that connects Edinburgh to Fife

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell hosts the country music show from the Town Hall in Newry, Co Down, featuring Charlie Landsborough and his special guest Mairéad Ní Mhaonaigh as they take the stage

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Lincoln sort the treasures from their trash. Items include a diverse assortment of China, jewellery and ornaments, and a Chinese dish bought at a car-boot sale, which makes back more than 1,000 times what the owner paid for it

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of five family members answer questions about top 10 lists, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round. Presented by Sally Lindsay

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Eva, Jack, Emma and Gary pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.desc: Brenda accuses Faith, Mandy and Sam coach Lydia, and Liv is tempted

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: While Abi and Kevin enjoy their respective hen and stag parties, Asha and Summer discover they have been partying in a stolen car, and Seb and Nina suffer a vicious attack

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets more dogs struggling to find a home, including a golden retriever with an incontinence problem and a boisterous border collie with a bad hip - who needs Paul's help in the hydrotherapy centre. He also calls in on a tiny pregnant mongrel, and spends time with an OAP Patterdale who just needs a bit of company in her twilight years

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy is stunned to discover that Nina sustained several blows to her head and is undergoing an operation. Asha reels at the news of the assault, while Corey acts shocked

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Worried sick about Seb, Abi asks Kevin to cancel their wedding. Corey covers his tracks by telling Kelly to say nothing, while Asha is oblivious to their involvement in the attack

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Part two of two. Cameras focus on the criminals, some of who have even targeted friends and family members, by stealing their cash. Reporter Adnan Sarwar also investigates the romance con artists netting millions online by pretending to be a new love

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Corey swears to Asha that he is innocent, but it would be best if she tells the police that he was with her all evening, while Nina struggles to remember anything about the attack

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents a special edition of the matchmaking show, celebrating its tenth series and 10 years since the first edition of its parent show, Taken Out, aired in Australia. In a twist on the usual format, Paddy gives 30 men who have previously appeared on the show a chance to take a seat 'behind the lights', while a selection of female celebrities replace the boys in the 'Love Lift'. Among the stars taking part are The Only Way is Essex star Chloe Sims, and Olympic gymnast Claudia Fragapane. The host also catches up with some of the couples who found lasting love after meeting on the show

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The residents of Quahog become convinced Stewie is possessed by the Devil, forcing the Griffins to flee to Texas and move in with Lois's sister. The malevolent baby then dresses up as a girl to avoid suspicions about his true identity and enters a local beauty pageant

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys gatecrash a posh banquet having received an invitation by mistake. programme.title: Plebs

Jason and Aurelius both join male escort agency the Knights of Eros

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's performance at work suffers after he injures his hand in a fireworks accident, so Lois volunteers to help him out. programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's performance at work suffers after he injures his hand in a fireworks accident, so Lois volunteers to help him out. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Charles investigates the fatal stabbing of a businessman in a pub car park - and gradually discovers the victim was not the upstanding pillar of the community he appeared to be. Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce star

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is rushed to hospital unconscious, and Steve worries that he has killed him. Vera refuses to let Jack smoke a pipe and Martin suggests he tries hypnotherapy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tries to remain optimistic about Jim's chances of recovery. Fred catches Zoe stealing. Des and Natalie go on a date together, and she lets him know she is interested in him

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a hotel room and the cash he was carrying has gone missing. Ronnie and Sam discover the victim owed his ex-wife thousands of pounds in child support for their terminally ill son and had been lying to his girlfriend about his line of work. As the investigation progresses, the detectives focus their inquiries on the child's grandfather. Did he commit murder to recoup the unpaid money or is there more to this family set-up than first appears? Meanwhile, at the Crown Prosecution Service, Kate and Jake work out their differences. Guest starring Ian Bleasdale (Casualty)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Free from the restrictions of her position, Elizabeth is determined to bring the true murderer to some form of justice. Armed with information to solve the case, she heads straight into the heart of the viper's nest. In a disturbing confrontation, she is forced to face her own demons. Crime thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery and Charlotte Hope

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack's house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.desc: Kathy tells Graham how angry she is with Biff. Zak tries to talk Emily into speaking to Butch. Bernice receives some unwelcome financial news

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera takes Jack to Magenta, the hypnotherapist. Martin suggests to Ashley that Zoe should see a bereavement counsellor. Liz begins to worry that Jim will never recover

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley urges Zoe to seek professional help. Leanne receives a letter from Darren telling her he'll soon be out on parole. Vera is startled when Jack turns into Lusty Jack

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An idyllic summer day in Oxford is torn asunder when a parcel bomb claims the life of eminent mathematician Adam Capstone, a genius in the field of 'Knot Theory'. As Lewis and Hathaway investigate, they discover that, while the victim's academic reputation was beyond reproach, his private life was far more dubious. It emerges Capstone was a man of carnal appetites, who had a string of affairs with young students, betraying his chemistry professor wife Elizabeth, providing her with a clear motive to do away with her cheating spouse. As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam lead the investigation into the death of a wealthy woman whose body was dumped in the Thames. The team initially focuses inquiries on the victim's abusive ex-husband, with whom she had been involved in a bitter struggle over custody of their daughter Holly, but the case heads in a different direction when the detectives discover the teenager was infatuated with her mother's new partner. With Patrick Baladi and an appearance by Bradley Walsh's son Barney

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Zoe resents the Malletts for being a happy couple. Natalie accuses Des of using her to make Samantha jealous. Steve tells Fiona what happened between him and Jim

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve swears to Fiona he did not push Jim deliberately. Liz is thrilled when Jim moves his head. Samantha accuses Natalie of stealing Des from her

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A dark family secret comes to light when a female hit-and-run suspect claims the woman she deliberately ran over on a pedestrian crossing was a ghost. However, the facts of the case tell a different story. Gwen Taylor guest stars, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is shocked by the unexpected arrival of his ex-wife Maggie (Julie Graham), who appears to have forgotten that they separated years ago and wants to know why he left her. Eleanor goes on a date with local fisherman Paul Hale, an old friend she has not seen since school, while Martin and Louisa finally agree on a name for their baby - James Henry. With Martin Clunes, John Marquez and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Maggie gets the all-clear from the doctor and decides she will return to Bude, forcing PC Penhale to take desperate measures to persuade her to stay in Portwenn. Ruth uncovers a raft of family heirlooms while clearing out a barn at the farm, and the mounting tension between Martin and Louisa peaks when she discovers he has booked a date for James Henry's christening without consulting her

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found dead in her flat after a break-in, leading Ronnie and Sam to suspect a burglary gone wrong. However, following a tip-off from the victim's grand-daughter Connie, the detectives' attention turns to live-in carer Cecile, who is promptly arrested. But the accused points the finger back at Connie, claiming she had been deliberately starving her grandmother. Who will the team believe? With Jan Francis, Daniel Casey and Ramon Tikaram

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera plans to turn the Rovers into a 'no smoking' pub. Judy tells Zoe that she's pregnant. Mike offers Deirdre the job of running the office at the factory

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly and Roy decide to move into Viv's to save money. Graham confides in Ashley that Rachel is dead because of him. Bernice's Camelot Night gets underway

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff asks for Marlon's advice about how to propose to Kathy. Zak has a man-to-man chat with Butch about women. Kathy discovers a suicide note from Graham and tries to find him

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kate meets a couple trying to raise the money to send their eight-year-old son to a Swiss sanatorium to cure his TB, Greengrass is persuaded to play Santa, and a spate of Christmas tree thefts hits Aidensfield. Sixties police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert decides that Angie, Ida and himself should all go on a diet

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Charles investigates the fatal stabbing of a businessman in a pub car park - and gradually discovers the victim was not the upstanding pillar of the community he appeared to be. Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mayhem and disorder erupts in the village when stolen loot from a robbery resurfaces

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff discovers that Graham stayed the night at Kathy's. Kathy and Graham talk about his attempt to take his life. Tricia wonders why Bernice is more popular than her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff tries to make things up with Kathy. Tricia plans her Hollywood evening at the pub. Emily runs away from Butch and tells him to stay away from her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is rushed to hospital unconscious, and Steve worries that he has killed him. Vera refuses to let Jack smoke a pipe and Martin suggests he tries hypnotherapy

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz tries to remain optimistic about Jim's chances of recovery. Fred catches Zoe stealing. Des and Natalie go on a date together, and she lets him know she is interested in him

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A man is found shot dead in a hotel room and the cash he was carrying has gone missing. Ronnie and Sam discover the victim owed his ex-wife thousands of pounds in child support for their terminally ill son and had been lying to his girlfriend about his line of work. As the investigation progresses, the detectives focus their inquiries on the child's grandfather. Did he commit murder to recoup the unpaid money or is there more to this family set-up than first appears? Meanwhile, at the Crown Prosecution Service, Kate and Jake work out their differences. Guest starring Ian Bleasdale (Casualty)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Free from the restrictions of her position, Elizabeth is determined to bring the true murderer to some form of justice. Armed with information to solve the case, she heads straight into the heart of the viper's nest. In a disturbing confrontation, she is forced to face her own demons. Crime thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery and Charlotte Hope

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack's house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An idyllic summer day in Oxford is torn asunder when a parcel bomb claims the life of eminent mathematician Adam Capstone, a genius in the field of 'Knot Theory'. As Lewis and Hathaway investigate, they discover that, while the victim's academic reputation was beyond reproach, his private life was far more dubious. It emerges Capstone was a man of carnal appetites, who had a string of affairs with young students, betraying his chemistry professor wife Elizabeth, providing her with a clear motive to do away with her cheating spouse. As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam lead the investigation into the death of a wealthy woman whose body was dumped in the Thames. The team initially focuses inquiries on the victim's abusive ex-husband, with whom she had been involved in a bitter struggle over custody of their daughter Holly, but the case heads in a different direction when the detectives discover the teenager was infatuated with her mother's new partner. With Patrick Baladi and an appearance by Bradley Walsh's son Barney

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: First episode of the fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon. Three agents of an international intelligence organisation are forced to crash-land in the Himalayas - where they receive superhuman powers from a mysterious stranger. Felix Aylmer and Burt Kwouk guest

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Suspected felon Billy Medhurst returns from Spain, and Regan determines to prove his involvement in an earlier robbery in which a Sweeney office was shot and left permanently disabled. Police drama, starring John Thaw, Dennis Waterman and Anthony Heaton

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo learn the origin of an old feud, and investigate a protection racket run by a vicious gang boss. Drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle have reason to suspect their former mentor has turned 'rogue' for money after two key suspects in a drug-smuggling case are delivered to their killers. Action drama, with Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Glyn Owen

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's niece is set to be married and he has agreed to supply the wedding limousine - but her big day is placed in jeopardy when he finds he also needs the vehicle to shift a consignment of pornographic magazines. Warren Clarke guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The team looks into irregularities in the international money market and uncovers a sinister, far-reaching mind-control conspiracy. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey return with the competitive angling show, and are joined in the Cotswolds by boxing legends Ricky Hatton and Johnny Nelson

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day one of the May Meeting, including the 2.15 Cheshire Oaks and the 3.15 Chester Vase. Presented by Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani, with analysis from Jason Weaver and Hayley Turner, reports by Matt Chapman and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: Goals from the stars of the 70s, 80s and 90s, including Kevin Keegan, Paul Gascoigne, Glenn Hoddle and Kenny Dalglish

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The team need to protect the only eye-witness of a drug-running racket. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur looks forward to a big payout when an ex-con needs his help to recover the loot from a robbery committed 14 years earlier - but learns banks are not always as safe as they seem. Vintage comedy drama, guest starring Rula Lenska, Maurice Denman and Max Wall

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey are joined by snooker star Jimmy White and darts player Bobby George for some fishing fun in Oxfordshire

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Radio 2 presenter Ken Bruce, former England midfielder Paul Ince, EastEnders actress Laila Morse (Mo Harris) and boxer David Haye compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson's comic alter ego embarks on another disaster-filled outing when he treats his hotel room to a makeover but ends up causing devastation

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: The bumbling hero tries his level best to turn a babysitting job into a fun-packed day at the fair, but predictably makes a hash of it. Slapstick comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Total Recall (1990)
programme.desc: A construction worker pays to have fake memories of a dream holiday implanted into his brain, but discovers his mind has already been tampered with - apparently to erase his true identity as a secret agent from a colony on Mars. He journeys to the planet in search of the truth, but is drawn into a conflict between the tyrannical ruler and a rebel faction. Paul Verhoeven's sci-fi thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Rachel Ticotin and Michael Ironside

However, his paranoia and cocaine addiction begin to spiral out of control, while his enemies circle, waiting for the chance to bring him down. Gangster thriller remake, with Al Pacino, Michelle Pfeiffer and Robert Loggia

programme.title: Total Recall (1990)
programme.desc: A construction worker pays to have fake memories of a dream holiday implanted into his brain, but discovers his mind has already been tampered with - apparently to erase his true identity as a secret agent from a colony on Mars. Paul Verhoeven's sci-fi thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Rachel Ticotin and Michael Ironside

programme.title: Scarface (1983)
programme.desc: Small-time crook Tony Montana emigrates from Cuba to the US, determined to become rich and powerful. Admitted into the inner circle of a Miami drug lord, he ultimately takes over his boss's empire and even his wife. However, his paranoia and cocaine addiction begin to spiral out of control, while his enemies circle, waiting for the chance to bring him down. Gangster thriller remake, with Al Pacino, Michelle Pfeiffer and Robert Loggia

programme.desc: Two sets of house-hunters look for properties in north London. Kirstie Allsopp is with single mum and businesswoman Digi, who has £1.25million to find the perfect place for her, daughter Emily and mum Queelan. However, it soon proves a challenge to find the family home that suits all their needs. Phil Spencer helps Rebecca and Sohail, who have raised their family in rented accommodation and now want a place of their own now. programme.title: First Dates Hotel

Meanwhile, Darill quickly learns there are certain things you should keep to yourself when he sits down with single mum Millie. Other pairings include part-time actor Simon and Disney-loving fellow thespian James, and 83-year-old former jazz singer Dorit and 82-year-old poetry lover John

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: Glamping is on the rise in Britain, and it's no longer just a posh tent in a field. Up and down the country people are repurposing old vehicles into boutique hotels - and now, there's a new kid in town. This documentary follows comedian Johnny Vegas and his assistant Bev see through their plans to create the county's coolest glamping site. Johnny is starting by buying a vintage bus he has seen online, hoping to renovate it and put it in a field as part of his new project. The only problem is, the vehicle is in Malta, so he will need a plane to collect it

programme.title: The Great Garden Revolution
