programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane moves in with Frasier, but unfortunately soon develops an allergic reaction to his dog. Comedy, starring Shelley Long and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm decides to make some major changes in his life after a chest X-ray reveals a worrying spot - but he does not seem ready to start living like a saint. Bar-room comedy, starring George Wendt and Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert finally decides to propose to Amy and persuades Ray to accompany him to meet her parents - who prove far from amenable to the idea. Comedy, starring Monica Horn, Brad Garrett and Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra urges Ray to be firmer with the twins, but his heavy-handed approach to discipline soon has her backtracking. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray reluctantly agrees to do charity work at a hospital, but proves so popular with the patients that he starts spending more time with them than at home - much to wife Debra's dismay. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton star

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frederick announces he is unable to visit at Christmas, prompting a dejected Frasier to volunteer to work over the festive period. Unfortunately, he soon finds his callers are even more depressed than he is, and seeks solace in a late-night diner. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin spends a romantic evening with a neighbour - to the surprise of Frasier, who causes family friction by discussing their date on his radio show. Meanwhile, Roz goes out with a Star Trek fan. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin persuades Frasier to take part in a charity auction, where he is thrilled to be bought by a model - but all she wants him to do is babysit her daughter. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Rebecca set to work renovating their kitchen, bBut with big dreams and a limited budget Rebecca visits Angel to see how far their money can stretch with a bit of creative know-how. Amy and Marc set themselves the unenviable task of transforming a bedroom with damaged floors, crumbling walls and a bathroom stuck in a '70s time-warp into a luxury B&B suite. Ben and Billie have it all to do as they aim to revamp an old outbuilding into a Moroccan oasis and illuminate the chateau with an architectural lighting display in preparation for Billie's brother's wedding

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Married couple Lynne and Ian kick off the competition in Gateshead, Tyne and Wear, with a menu of hearty treats. On day two newly-weds Jess and Connor cook up a Greek meal - including a rather large mezze - followed by a Greek dance and some plate smashing. After two high-energy dinner parties, engaged couple Tom and Sam's chances rest on their banana souffle

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with Samira Ahmed joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Neil are about to start their next big adventure and move from their home in Exeter to start a new life in Lecce in southern Italy. They want a property that has a guest annex to accommodate friends and family, and that's close to the beach so Neil can surf whenever he wants. Armed with a budget of £200,000, Jasmine Harman shows them five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix embarks on a road trip around some of Europe's most beautiful locations, meeting the Brits who have set up lives abroad. In the first edition, he sets out on a tour of southern Spain's whitewashed villages in Andalucía, where three sets of expats each run beautiful boutique hotels. He meets a couple with a grand plan to host weddings, and another learning on the job. Plus, can a single mum juggle work and family?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Beach House in Portland, Dorset, where Pete Lacey feels his back-to-basics approach offers his guests good value. However, things get heated with the radiator in Karen and Liam's room and opinions are laid bare over dinner

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Jaya Mistry spices up the competition, challenging chef Frederick Forster to her family Indian recipe to help her win the £10,000 prize. She's keen to curry favour with the food jury, but when it comes to baking chef's apple tart, there is a possibility her dreams of a win may crumble. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After making a mess of a speech at work, Homer tries to bolster his public speaking skills by turning to improvisational comedy. Marge decides to upgrade Bart's tumbledown treehouse, but tensions arise when Bart questions her building skills

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Summer takes drastic measures to stop Warren seeing his children, and Luke's mum Sue makes it clear that she thinks Mandy is a more suitable match for her son than Cindy

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Married couple Dexter and Aaron are looking for a fluffy, chunky dog. Ellie the chihuahua will have to prove that being neither fluffy or chunky isn't a problem when it comes to being their perfect match. Yoga teacher Venessa and son Jacob try to assess whether there is a bond between them and Zoe, a friendly beagle who has been trying to sniff out a new home for a while. Plus, Stanley the anxious Staffie meets 'dog-mad' pair Linda and Richard

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks to Sarah Kendall, Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack and Mike Wozniak. Sarah still leads the race to win Greg's stupendous skull-trophy, but the chasing pack are closing in, hoping beyond hope to score points by creatively munching on snacks, drawing pretty wings and being brutally honest about emergency plane landings. As usual, Alex Horne offers the intrepid contenders moral support

programme.title: Frank of Ireland
programme.desc: When Frank discovers that Padraig has recruited Doofus to write songs for an am-dram performance of Twelve Angry Women: The Musical, he's resolves to share the limelight and muscle in on Doofus's set list. However, as opening night approaches, Frank's songs aren't getting the positive response he'd hoped for, while Doofus's material is going down a storm. Comedy, with Brian Gleeson, Domhnall Gleeson, Pom Boyd, Sarah Greene and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes the oldest ever picker to the studio, 75-year-old naturist Ian from North Yorkshire. Ian has recently been exploring his sexuality and needs help to find his perfect partner. Next up is 30-year-old waitress Kerry from Aberdeen, who finds the smell of men's armpits gets her hot under the collar and wants a man with a sweet scent who can also get experimental in the bedroom

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable dates and other moments from the First Dates Hotel, including the meeting between policewoman Emma and carpenter Luke, which promised to be one of the most passionate dates in the show's history. Meanwhile, Darill quickly learns there are certain things you should keep to yourself when he sits down with single mum Millie. Other pairings include part-time actor Simon and Disney-loving fellow thespian James, and 83-year-old former jazz singer Dorit and 82-year-old poetry lover John

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Nashville, Tennessee, the spiritual home of country music, but his visit begins on a sour note at Chappy's Cajun restaurant. Chef John Chapman's careless methods have damaged his reputation, but he blames the local clientele rather than his less-than-appetising food and disturbing kitchen practices. Can Gordon get through to this stubborn owner and save the eatery from disaster?

programme.title: Trip Hazard: My Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Rosie Jones is joined by fellow comedian Jenny Eclair for an 'outdoorsy' adventure on the island of Anglesey in north Wales. The pair discover they'd make terrible farmers when they try their hand at sheep herding and visit the island's many Neolithic stone sites. Rosie takes Jenny on a horse ride along the waterfront, and they take to the skies on the world's fastest zip wire. As the sun sets, they meet a coast craft expert, who teaches them essential survival skills on one of the island's many picturesque beaches - and it's quickly evident they'd not last more than few hours if left to fend for themselves. Narrated by Olivia Colman

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from south Wales compete to host the best dinner party and walk away with the cash prize of £1,000. South African born Nigel and his Welsh wife Nikki kick off the competition with dishes from Nigel's homeland, while Darren and Simon attempt to woo their guests with a flavour of their first-class lifestyle on day two, before down-to-earth Neil and Carly offer up a taste of their favourite dishes on the final night

programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale
programme.desc: June escalates her risky efforts to find Hannah. Emily must face her past crimes as the ongoing international diplomatic crisis becomes more complicated. Serena and Fred contemplate their future in Washington, DC. Dystopian drama continuing the story of Margaret Atwood's original novel, starring Elisabeth Moss

programme.title: Baby Surgeons: Delivering Miracles
programme.desc: Three expectant mothers face dangerous complications in their pregnancies, and have to decide whether they are willing to undergo pioneering but potentially risky surgery for a successful birth. A woman carrying triplets shows signs of a rare blood condition that can cause miscarriages, another mother's foetus has become entangled in the lining of the womb and risks losing a foot, and a woman who is 36 weeks pregnant discovers that her baby has fluid building up in its brain

programme.title: The £100k Drop

programme.title: Coach Trip

programme.desc: Frasier is disillusioned to discover a colleague has fallen head over heels in love with Carla. Comedy, starring Ted Danson, Kelsey Grammer and Rhea Perlman

programme.desc: Amy's mother and father turn up unexpectedly, intending to take their daughter back home with them. They are less than happy to find Ray there, let alone his parents, and the atmosphere is tense as the families take an instant dislike to each other

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is too ill to take Ally to a pyjama party, so Ray reluctantly agrees to do the honours. Unfortunately it means coming face to face with Peggy, an old enemy who physically assaulted him during a charity event

programme.title: Frasier

programme.desc: It's breaking point for Tim and Rebecca as they struggle to complete their kitchen as an expensive mishap with granite worktops put Rebecca's dream room in jeopardy. Meanwhile, at Ben and Billie's chateau, the duo welcome 60 guests for the wedding of Billie's brother Pete

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, three couples compete to host the best dinner party. Sarah and Paddy kick things off with a fine-dining feast for guests Louise and Danny and Emma and Samson. The hosts mix Eastern and traditional fare. Then, on night two Emma and Samson treat their guests to some Mozambican delights. After a first course that whistles and wows, palates need to be cleansed for the main course, but a chilly chat about ice cream leaves Louise red in the face. On the final night, Louise and Danny confound everyone's expectations with a sailing-based starter. A spot of model behaviour excites the guests, and a mountain of meringue impresses, but is it enough to win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix's European tour moves on to his native France, where he explores the Bordeaux and Bergerac wine regions. He meets a pair of English winemakers who are trying to make a name for themselves, and a sommelier and his chef wife, who are launching a gîte and a cookery school

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Home cook Lulu Marodeen brings tinned salmon to take on Mark Sargeant. A battle of the bananas commences, as Michelin-starred chef Mark turns out a fruity tarte Tatin with ginger biscuits. Will Lulu be tasting sweet success with the jury or will soggy pastry leave her chances of £10,000 in tatters? Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Channel 4 News

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team tracks down fascinating facts from around the world related to the growing British tradition of savouring takeaway food, on which a billion pounds is spent a month. Jimmy Doherty is in Thailand to discover the unexpected addition in spring rolls. He then heads to India to find out the real reason takeaway curry can be so delicious - the mythical ingredient, ghee. Amanda Byram spills the beans on coffee and learns why some cups leave consumers more 'buzzing' than others. Andi Oliver has the meaty task of learning how doner kebabs are made and bags herself an invite to the fancy award ceremony known as the Kebaftas

programme.desc: Adam and Jonny arrive at Mum and Dad's house with big news to announce - they both have girlfriends. Dad buys a caravan that has seen better days and is keen for the family to have dinner in there. However, Mum refuses to step foot inside, though Jim is less fussy as his house has flooded. Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Tamsin Greig and Paul Ritter star in Robert Popper's award-winning comedy

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Robert Popper's low-key comedy returns with Jonny in a foul mood, as he discovers a secret betrayal while reading Adam's childhood diary - and vows to get revenge. Mum struggles to keep the peace as the brothers' feud rages through the house, while Dad's inability to control his sneezing causes further chaos. Starring Simon Bird, Tom Rosenthal, Tamsin Greig and Paul Ritter

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam tries to celebrate his birthday, but the evening is spoiled when Sheila Bloom, who is famously obsessed with her car, makes catty remarks about his vehicle. His father then reveals he used to date her years ago, upsetting Mum in the process. Finally, the family abandons dinner and heads to a Chinese restaurant, only for Sheila to reappear, forcing Dad to explain what the pair really got up to

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Grandma introduces the family to her new boyfriend, but the old curmudgeon quickly upsets everyone with his rude behaviour. Dad cannot stand him and hides in his shed, but nobody is prepared for what happens when the guest suddenly loses his temper. Comedy, starring Simon Bird

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: The boys come home to find the builders have been in and the house is in a complete mess. On top of this, there's a mouse loose and Mum takes refuge in the shed, leaving Dad in charge of making dinner. Jim pops round to see if he can help, and is invited for his first-ever Friday night dinner with the Goodmans. Comedy, with Mark Heap

programme.title: Frank of Ireland
programme.desc: When Frank discovers that Padraig has recruited Doofus to write songs for an am-dram performance of Twelve Angry Women: The Musical, he's resolves to share the limelight and muscle in on Doofus's set list. However, as opening night approaches, Frank's songs aren't getting the positive response he'd hoped for, while Doofus's material is going down a storm. Comedy, with Brian Gleeson, Domhnall Gleeson, Pom Boyd, Sarah Greene and Tom Vaughan-Lawlor

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he meets husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who reveal a shocking contempt for customer feedback. The chef faces the biggest challenge of the series to save Amy's Baking Company, but can he get through to this defiant pair?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition heads to Milton Keynes, where first to host is 52-year old hypnotherapist Howard, who is hoping his fancy menu will go down with his guests. However, with dishes including 'red treacle tart' his visitors are unsure what to expect, and not everyone shares the thrill when Howard introduces his 'Pendulum of Truth' to the proceedings

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Louise hosts the second night in Milton Keynes, where she hopes to wow her guests with her 'fabulous' menu - including bruschetta and mousse. However, the outspoken PA receives criticism from her guests - will she be able to overcome it to secure the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Management consultant David hosts the third dinner party in Milton Keynes, and his guests are impressed when he introduces some dancing Las Vegas showgirls. However, with all his focus on entertainment, David makes a major blunder with the food and serves vegetarian Helen a meat dish

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Customer services manager and vegetarian Helen hosts the fourth night of the competition in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, and hopes that an East meets West-themed night will win her the £1,000 prize. Hypnotherapist Howard is intrigued by some items he finds in her bedroom, while salesman Simon enjoys her entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final evening in Milton Keynes and the dinner party is thrown by salesman Simon, who believes that his night of fine dining will impress the critical guests. However, things begin to go awry when Howard embarks on a mission to throw his plans off course, and Helen discovers a collection of awards that evidence the host's competitive nature. Meanwhile, Louise finally comes clean about her dislike of David, before the votes are cast and the winner is announced

programme.desc: Grandpa Pig takes Peppa and George to the playground, but has trouble understanding the children's rules

programme.desc: When spirited rabbit Pip tries to help sweet-natured mouse Posy, he cannot help showing off his roller skating skills - making Posy give up altogether. Pip has to find a way to get her back on her skates

programme.desc: Mr Gruber is loaned a T-Rex skeleton, only for Lucky the dog to steal a priceless bone and bury it somewhere

programme.desc: Hopper and Boo are both aces when it comes to hitting a target, so face off to a win very cool racing bicycle

With a wedding due in a few weeks, the completion date looms ominously

programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice offers advice to a Perthshire woman whose split-level bungalow in Crieff has been on the market for five years. Ann thinks too much clutter is the problem, and quickly sets to work redecorating the hallway, kitchen and lounge, before moving on to the front garden to increase its 'kerb appeal'

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice and Alistair Appleton ride to the rescue of a couple who are having trouble selling their converted chapel in Yeovil, Somerset. Happily, a lick of paint and the eviction of a resident rat help transform the dingy interior into a cool, airy space

programme.desc: Mia and Ari reminisce about their lost child, Nikau cannot handle Ryder and Chloe's loved-up state, and John has to deal with the world once again

programme.title: Killer Secrets (2019)
programme.desc: Single mother Kerry returns to her home town after receiving news that her estranged father has died. She and her daughter Ella intend to remain until the funeral, but after Ella falls in love with the town, the two decide to stay. Kerry hires a former classmate-turned-contractor to fix up her old house, but the more 'fixing' he does on his childhood crush's house, the more lethal mishaps occur. Thriller, starring Alyshia Ochse and Zac Titus

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Self-confessed workaholic and diet entrepreneur Terri-Ann Nunn and her family exchange homes, budgets and lives with accident-repair driver Mick Ross, his wife Anne and their children. The Nunns live in a luxury six-bedroom home, complete with hot tub and gym, in west Doncaster, whereas the Rosses rent a three-bedroom council house in the east of the town. How will Terri-Ann cope with putting her business to the side - and more importantly, how will she manage without her mobile for a week?

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.desc: A mysterious woman arrives, offering help to Ned at The Hive. Susan refuses to choose between her two very passionate teachers

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mia and Ari reminisce about their lost child, Nikau cannot handle Ryder and Chloe's loved-up state, and John has to deal with the world once again

programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London
programme.desc: The chief yeoman warder oversees the tower's celebration of the end of the First World War, and special preparations are made for the tower director's retirement party, involving a military band and a unique farewell as he leaves the tower for the last time. Newly hatched raven George is introduced to the rest of the birds, not all of whom take kindly to newcomers, and historian Tracy Borman unearths the story of Roger Casement, who was executed for treason for supporting Irish independence during the First World War

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Following police on a manhunt for a dangerous suspect, as dog-handler Jen and crime-fighting canine Quantum search in the dark for the assailant. A crackdown on cannabis cultivation leads police to £500,000 worth of Class B drugs farmed in a disused warehouse, and on the roads, a van with false plates causes complete carnage

programme.title: Here Come the Gypsies!
programme.desc: Gypsy scrap metal worker John finds himself in a tight spot when the falling price of scrap leaves him short of cash to buy a special gift for his daughter. Meanwhile at the Durham fair, gypsies have gathered from miles around to celebrate their culture and make money from horses, and two traders do battle selling carts and harnesses

programme.title: Police Raids: Caught by Surprise
programme.desc: Cameras follow enforcement agencies carrying out raids across Britain, as they take drastic action against fraudsters, counterfeiters and criminals. Police tackle the brothers responsible for a major drug-dealing operation in south Wales, while sniffer dogs Yoyo and Scamp track illegal and possibly dangerous cigarettes

programme.title: Inside the Force: 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at Lincoln's West Parade police station, beginning by focusing on the custody suite as a procession of challenging suspects is brought in. programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines the role of the railways during the two global conflicts of the 20th century, keeping vital supply lines open and transporting troops to and from the front. In the First World War, railway works were converted into munitions factories and women were employed for these dangerous jobs and their heroic efforts helped win them the vote, while in the Second World War, they were vital in organising the evacuation of a million children and offered underground shelters from air raids

programme.desc: Self-confessed workaholic and diet entrepreneur Terri-Ann Nunn and her family exchange homes, budgets and lives with accident-repair driver Mick Ross, his wife Anne and their children. The Nunns live in a luxury six-bedroom home, complete with hot tub and gym, in west Doncaster, whereas the Rosses rent a three-bedroom council house in the east of the town. How will Terri-Ann cope with putting her business to the side - and more importantly, how will she manage without her mobile for a week?

programme.desc: At Glebe Cottage in Devon, Carol Klein shows viewers how to grow some of the nation's favourite soft fruit, including raspberries and blackcurrants. The host also explains how to plant and care for daffodils

programme.title: Escape to the Farm with Kate Humble
programme.desc: Almost a decade after planting 1,000 trees around Kate's Monmouthshire farm, it's time to thin the young trees. She has brought her friend, Kate Mobbs-Morgan and her powerful Ardennes horse Kip, to clear the trees in a more traditional and forgiving way. In the kitchen, Kate starts the day with a poached duck egg with green chillies on a toasted crumpet, then cooks a hearty Italian-style woodsman's pasta with porcini mushrooms and pancetta

programme.title: Milly Dowler: The Botched Investigation
programme.desc: This true crime documentary about the case of Milly Dowler looks in new detail at the role of the media, the police investigation into her disappearance and Levi Bellfield, the man who was eventually convicted of her murder. Why did it take police so long to look at Bellfield, who lived just yards from where Milly was last seen? And what was behind the tabloids' hacking of Milly's phone?

programme.title: White House Farm Murders: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Examining the case of Jeremy Bamber, who was convicted of murdering both of his parents, his sister and her six-year-old twin sons at the family farmhouse in Essex in August 1985. The programme analyses the events of that fateful night, the subsequent investigation, and what was - and wasn't - presented at the trial of Bamber, asking whether someone else could have killed the family at White House Farm

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A woman arrives by air ambulance after falling 30ft off a castle wall and is rushed to the CT scanner to check for any life-threatening internal injuries, while a 68-year-old man in Minors is losing a lot of blood after cutting his hand on a broken glass at home. Dr Ibraheem treats a 25-year-old who has had a nasty accident while out walking his dog, with X-rays showing that he has ripped his shoulder out of joint

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary showing how CCTV and other technological advances are helping to bring car thieves to justice. The programme also includes film of a getaway in which robbers tried to evade the law by driving at 180mph

programme.desc: Chris examines the devastating impact of the Beeching report, which led to half the country's stations and almost a third of the lines being closed. programme.title: World's Most Scenic River Journeys
programme.desc: A journey along Ireland's longest river, the Shannon, through an emerald green landscape of rolling hills and sparkling waters. Carving a route from north to south of the country, we discover how Vikings, monks and kings have all made their mark along this important waterway. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Revealed: Cleopatra's Lost City
programme.desc: The results of a three-decade investigation to uncover one of ancient Egypt's greatest lost cities, Canopus, which for centuries had been lost beneath the waves. It's a city connected with Cleopatra, as well as with four great ancient civilisations. It's a story that starts 10 miles off the Egyptian coast at Alexandria

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles narrates this look back at 1989, the era of Madonna, Milli Vanilli and Jason Donovan. Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini, Cheryl Baker and Jenny Powell add their thoughts on the year when the Bangles' Eternal Flame was a huge hit, Lisa Stansfield sang All Around The World, and Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers were almost unstoppable

programme.title: That's So
programme.desc: A look at the events of 1989, which saw Ayatollah Khomeini call for the death of writer Salman Rushdie following the publication of his controversial novel The Satanic Verses, the fall of the Berlin Wall, the Hillsborough tragedy, the televised Tiananmen Square crackdown and music by Black Box, Cher, Madonna and a youthful Take That

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. In Hammersmith, west London, camera operators help police as they try to enforce a drinking ban on a local park and spot a group whose members may be about to break into a shop

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica's rich boyfriend embarks on a bid to become the Ultimate Fighting Champion. Chandler feels his boss is becoming a little too friendly, and the gang is delighted to overhear a scandalous conversation between two familiar-looking men in Central Perk. Comedy, guest starring Robin Williams and Billy Crystal

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A mini-heatwave results in an influx of patients at Barnsley Hospital. 50-year-old Mark is rushed into the emergency department after an accident involving a garden shredder, while three-year-old Ophelia is brought in by her dad after a mishap with a sun cream bottle. Elsewhere, doctors treat 64-year-old Melvin, who suffers from the incurable lung condition COPD, and 27-year-old Jack is assessed after collapsing at work

programme.title: Nick's Quest
programme.desc: Nick Baker highlights the plight of South American Orinoco crocodiles as he documents a conservation project in Venezuela to save the species from extinction. In addition to heading out on a dangerous night-time hunt to find out how the creatures are surviving in the wild, he tries to coax a three-year-old to return to the open river

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: A fox cub shows symptoms of a life-threatening condition, and a young badger reacts badly to goat's milk

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice helps a Staffordshire couple sell their three-bedroom house in Newcastle-under-Lyme by clearing clutter and creating more space. Her efforts pay off, and with fresher shades on the walls and a laminated floor, they soon find a buyer for the property

programme.title: O.S.S. (1946)
programme.desc: Alan Ladd is on fine form as a tough spy sent to France to collect vital information shortly before D-Day. His team of expert operatives includes a strong-minded female agent - but sparks fly when the pair begin to work together, a situation which looks likely to lead to a crisis of conscience. By-the-numbers World War Two adventure, with Geraldine Fitzgerald, Patric Knowles and Richard Benedict

programme.title: Destry Rides Again (1939)
programme.desc: A mild-mannered sheriff is assigned to clean up a lawless town ruled over by a ruthless gangster - but finds his biggest problem is a sultry saloon singer determined to exact revenge on him for resisting her charms. Comedy Western, starring James Stewart, Marlene Dietrich, Charles Winninger, Mischa Auer and Brian Donlevy

programme.title: Ministry of Fear (1944)
programme.desc: An innocent man recently released from a mental hospital inadvertently takes possession of a piece of microfilm meant for a Nazi agent, and finds himself on the run from both the police and the Nazis. Spy thriller based on Graham Greene's novel, starring Ray Milland, Marjorie Reynolds, Carl Esmond and Hillary Brooke

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen (1960)
programme.desc: A former Army colonel, angry at his enforced retirement, sets out to exact an elaborate revenge on society in general. Recruiting seven other former servicemen who have turned to a life of crime, he plans a major bank robbery with military precision. Crime caper, starring Jack Hawkins, Nigel Patrick, Richard Attenborough, Roger Livesey and Bryan Forbes

programme.title: Walking Out (2017)
programme.desc: An urban teenager journeys to Montana to hunt big game with his estranged father. The duo struggle to connect, until a brutal encounter in the wilderness changes everything. Adventure, starring Matt Bomer, Josh Wiggins and Bill Pullman

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Rogue Nation (2015)
programme.desc: The team of secret agents faces being shut down when the head of the CIA convinces the US Senate that it is too dangerous. The operatives of the Impossible Mission Force become fugitives when they defy orders and plot to bring down a shadowy society of assassins. Spy thriller sequel, starring Tom Cruise, Jeremy Renner, Rebecca Ferguson and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Total Recall (2012)
programme.desc: A man living in a bleak future years after a nuclear war has fake memories of being a secret agent implanted into his brain. However, the procedure uncovers the fact that he really is a spy with his memory erased, and he is forced to become a fugitive from the totalitarian government. Sci-fi thriller remake, with Colin Farrell, Kate Beckinsale and Jessica Biel

programme.title: 10 Cloverfield Lane (2016)
programme.desc: A woman survives a car crash, and wakes up to find herself in an underground bunker, belonging to a survivalist who claims that a devastating attack has left the outside world uninhabitable. As his behaviour becomes increasingly stranger, she begins to fear she is being tricked and resolves to discover the truth for herself. Thriller, starring Mary Elizabeth Winstead and John Goodman

programme.title: Border River (1954)
programme.desc: A Confederate officer takes part in a daring mission to buy weapons to aid in the hard-fought battles against the Yankees, but things go awry, forcing his one true love to save him. Western, starring Joel McCrea, Yvonne de Carlo, Pedro Armendariz and Howard Petrie

programme.title: Anne of the Thousand Days (1969)
programme.desc: Henry VIII wins the hand of his ill-fated second wife Anne Boleyn by divorcing his first queen Katherine of Aragon, leading to a cataclysmic rift between the English Crown and the Vatican. However, when the marriage fails to produce a male heir, Henry and his council bring allegations of adultery, incest and treason against Anne, and condemn her to death. Historical drama, starring Richard Burton, Genevieve Bujold, Anthony Quayle, Michael Hordern and Irene Papas

programme.title: The Bells of St Mary's (1945)
programme.desc: Priest Chuck O'Malley comes to the aid of a nun running a parochial school in financial difficulty. While the pair rarely see eye to eye, they pool their resources to improve conditions for the pupils. Musical drama sequel to Going My Way, with Bing Crosby reprising his Oscar-winning role alongside Ingrid Bergman, Henry Travers and Joan Carroll

programme.title: Now You See Me 2 (2016)
programme.desc: A gang of magicians-turned-criminals is reunited for a comeback performance by a ruthless technology magnate, whose actual goal is to force the team into carrying out a seemingly impossible heist on his behalf. The illusionists realise their only hope of avoiding the law is to perform one last unprecedented stunt to expose the businessman's illegal activities. Crime thriller sequel, starring Jesse Eisenberg, Mark Ruffalo, Daniel Radcliffe and Morgan Freeman

programme.title: Dirty Grandpa (2016)
programme.desc: An uptight lawyer preparing to marry his boss's controlling daughter and secure a prestigious job is persuaded to drive his fun-loving grandfather to Florida. However, the old man is convinced his grandson is about to make a terrible mistake, and turns the road trip into a wildly hedonistic weekend in the hope of inspiring him to get more fun out of life. Comedy, starring Robert De Niro and Zac Efron

programme.title: Midnight Run (1988)
programme.desc: When accountant Jonathan Mardukas skips bail after having been arrested for embezzlement, bounty hunter Jack Walsh is hired to catch the fraudster and return him from New York to Los Angeles. Unfortunately, his task is hindered by the ingenuity of Mardukas and the attentions of pursuing FBI agents and Mafia hitmen. Comedy thriller, starring Robert De Niro, Charles Grodin, Yaphet Kotto, Dennis Farina, John Ashton and Joe Pantoliano

programme.title: The Taking of Pelham 123 (1974)
programme.desc: A ruthless criminal and his gang hijack a crowded New York subway train and demand a $1million ransom, threatening to shoot a passenger every minute after the deadline expires. As the deadline looms, a transit police detective struggles to cut through City Hall red tape and negotiate the safe release of the innocent people on board. Thriller, starring Walter Matthau, Robert Shaw, Martin Balsam, Hector Elizondo, Earl Hindman and James Broderick

programme.title: Flying Tigers (1942)
programme.desc: An American pilot leads a squadron of volunteer aviators to help China's fledgling air force fight the Japanese during the Second World War. However, he has to contend with the consequences when the actions of an arrogant hotshot pilot lead to the death of a fellow veteran airman. Drama, starring John Wayne, John Carroll, Anna Lee and Paul Kelly

programme.title: The Tin Star (1957)
programme.desc: A world-weary bounty hunter, formerly a sheriff, arrives in a small town where a newly-appointed lawman is struggling to keep the peace. When he learns of the mercenary's past, the inexperienced lawman seeks his help in learning the tricks of the trade so he can bring an outlaw gang to justice. Anthony Mann's Western, starring Henry Fonda, Anthony Perkins, Lee Van Cleef, Betsy Palmer and Michel Ray

programme.title: The Pink Panther (1963)
programme.desc: The world's most inept police detective, Inspector Clouseau, heads off to Switzerland in search of a suave jewel thief who has purloined a priceless diamond from a princess - but the bumbling officer's every ruse to foil the crime ends in chaos, not least when he is framed for the robbery himself. Blake Edwards' comedy, starring Peter Sellers, David Niven, Capucine, Robert Wagner and Claudia Cardinale

programme.title: A Shot in the Dark (1964)
programme.desc: The bungling Inspector Clouseau is sent to investigate a high-profile murder case. All the clues point to a maid being the guilty party, but the accident-prone detective is instantly smitten with her, and devotes his efforts to proving her innocence - despite all evidence to the contrary. Comedy, starring Peter Sellers, Elke Sommer, George Sanders and Herbert Lom

programme.title: Table 19 (2017)
programme.desc: Dumped by the best man via text, a former maid of honour decides to pitch up at her friend's wedding anyway. She finds herself relegated to table 19, where her tablemates include a nanny, an openly warring married couple, a teenager seeking a date and a gangly Englishman who's clearly hiding a secret. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Craig Robinson, Lisa Kudrow and Stephen Merchant

programme.title: Hunter Killer (2018)
programme.desc: Unconventional submarine skipper Joe Glass is sent to investigate the torpedoing of US and Russian vessels. He winds up trying to rescue the Russian president, who has been kidnapped by a rogue general. Action thriller, starring Gerard Butler, Gary Oldman and Common

programme.desc: When Dre and Rainbow take Zoey on a trip to visit Rainbow's old university, Dre's fear of flying kicks in and he needs a little assistance to get him through the flight. Charlie ends up babysitting the other youngsters and tells them about Dominique, an old flame of his who has come back into the picture

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Before the family members have their Christmas card photo taken, Dre takes Andre Jr and Jack to get their hair cut by the barber he has used for his entire life. However, when Andre Jr decides to let another barber cut his hair, Dre thinks his son has violated a sacred tradition. Sitcom, starring Anthony Anderson

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The squad members head to the annual Tri-State Cop-Con event, which is usually their excuse for a big party. However, their fun is threatened when Captain Holt asks them to be on their best behaviour.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Amy vanishes shortly before she is due to take her sergeant's exam, leaving Jake and Rosa with just a few hours to find her and bring her back to sit the test before it is too late. US cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg and Melissa Furnero

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny ask Howard and Bernadette for advice when they argue about money. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Raj try to find out whether they could survive a dark matter research expedition in a salt mine by attempting to simulate the conditions in a steam tunnel beneath the university. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is left in a difficult professional situation when a doctor (guest star Billy Bob Thornton) turns up at her door following her flirtatious sales technique, so the guys try to help her out. Meanwhile, Amy and Bernadette have a dispute over whether female scientists should flaunt their sexuality to try to get ahead at work. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Cleo must think fast when she fears that a hospital patient is in danger. Meanwhile,Sienna confronts Maxine, and Trish has a difficult decision to make

programme.title: Ackley Bridge
programme.desc: Kaneez is initially furious to discover that Nas is considering the arranged marriage, but the pressure of planning the wedding and her web of lies leads Nas to break down to her mum and confess that she is gay. While focusing on helping Missy get Hayley back and also straightening out Simone, Emma neglects Chloe on her birthday, sending Chloe off the rails, while Steve battles with Mandy's indiscretion, leaving him wondering whether they have a future together after all

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Tensions rise when the guys struggle to complete their Air Force project on time and are forced to work around the clock - leaving Sheldon fighting to stay awake. Penny and Amy attend a university gathering at the home of Bert the geologist. Raj learns Bernadette's true feelings about her pregnancy when he helps her to clear out the spare room for a nursery

programme.title: The Inbetweeners 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Awkward teenagers Will, Simon and Neil find university life does not live up to their expectations, and are insanely jealous of Jay's stories of his gap year in Australia. Heading Down Under to meet their friend, they inevitably discover he has exaggerated his escapades - so decide to go in search of a real adventure. Comedy sequel, starring Simon Bird, James Buckley, Joe Thomas and Blake Harrison

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is bothered by the attention he receives in the wake of his heavy element discovery, but is taken aback when his work is disproved by Leonard. Meanwhile, Raj makes Howard and Bernadette feel inadequate when he stays with them for a week and is the perfect house guest

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon leaves for Texas when his sister goes into labour. While he is away, the rest of the friends decorate for Christmas and reflect on how much he has changed their lives. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, Judith, a 57-year-old breast cancer survivor from Nuneaton, and Rigby, a 27-year-old hot tub installer from Chesterfield, take part

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, 30-year-old Pablo, who is HIV positive and hopes to break the stigma about his condition, and Leon, a musician and rapper from Burnley, take part

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: TV's most vocal armchair critics give their thoughts on programmes from February 2020, including The Stranger, Love Is Blind, EastEnders, Crazy Delicious, Spy in the Wild, Hunted, and ITV News reports on Harry and Meghan, and brain surgery

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Rhod Gilbert is at the centre of some satsuma-based controversy and Phil Wang finds his Bruce Lee costume offers an unexpected advantage

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Zander and Ryan begin working on a graphic novel, but Lennox takes all the fun out of the project when she assumes some editorial responsibilities. Meanwhile, Mel attempts to persuade Joe to consider waiting before embarking on a physical relationship with his new partner. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel gets back in touch with a childhood friend, and as things progress romantically, Joe fears they could actually be relatives. Meanwhile, Lennox faces a moral dilemma when she discovers that her blog's new sponsor participates in unethical labour practices. Guest starring Trevor Donovan

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: When Adam and Don realise they need to bring in more business, they film a commercial advertising their company. Meanwhile, Andi works up the courage to ask her boss, Bob for a raise

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi set up Adam's boss on a date to boost her spirits in the hope that she will be in the mood to have Adam resume work on the mini-mall. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Danny is desperate to build bridges with Riley after their recent break-up, but she's still furious after learning that he hooked up with Sam in high school. Determined to prove that she's not overreacting, Riley puts Danny on 'trial' for breaking her heart, with Bonnie acting as Danny's lawyer. Meanwhile, Ben tries to save face with Zoey after discovering that her ex-boyfriend has returned and wants to get back together with her

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits Manhattan, where she creates candy canes and lollipops at artisan sugar crafter Papabubble, before heading back to the UK to learn how to make passion fruit marshmallows covered in chocolate and coconut

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Laura and Leah Wright from Surrey have been enjoying holidays together in Spain since Leah was a child. Laura has now decided to put the cash from a recent inheritance towards buying a holiday home on Spain's Orihuela Costa that they can enjoy throughout the year, so Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties for their budget of £65,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Henry Cole visit a motorbike fan, making table lamps from engines and turning a pallet and iron wheels into a stylish modern coffee table

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives visit a stately home in Warwickshire as they try to find relatives of a lady who was born there, and an heir on another case embarks on a journey of discovery to learn about the grandfather he never met. Plus, details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: After 34 years of teaching, one expat faces being married to the boss, while a British-run scuba diving business plans to move back to UK waters. Plus, the British owners of a bar in Spain's only desert face their busiest night yet

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off in Worcestershire, where friends Geri Atterbury and Lorraine Rigby invite guests to stay at Greenways of Malvern. On arrival, couple Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker are not impressed by the wild wallpaper, and the lack of an en-suite dismays mother-and-son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Royal Forester at Bewdley in Worcestershire for their second stay, with proprietors Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker hoping to impress their guests with a trip to a local safari park. Sarah and Luke are impressed with their colourful room, but Sanjeev and Elinor have a 'hairy' experience

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From Ferry Lodge in Holyhead, Anglesey, where mother-and-son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway hope to impress the other contestants. However, things do not get off to a good start when Geri and Lorraine are overwhelmed by the number of laminated signs, Sean and Maxine begrudge paying 50p for toiletries and Sanjeev and Elinor are left unimpressed by the cleanliness of the establishment

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Partners Sanjeev Bhagotra and Elinor Roberts welcome their guests to Court Colman Manor, Bridgend, Glamorgan. Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker are pleased with the size of their room on arrival, but Sarah Allaway discovers a collection of cobwebs in hers. During the afternoon, the groups take a lesson in fire-making, and the next morning a traditional Welsh breakfast is not to everyone's taste - with the hosts wondering whether tactical voting has played a part come feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet one last time to find out what they have been paid. Geri kicks off proceedings by demanding to know why Sarah and Luke did not want a return visit to Greenways of Malvern, before they in turn confront Sanjeev and Elinor over their low cleanliness score for Ferry Lodge. Then the Court Colman Manor proprietors accuse Sarah and Luke of unfairly judging their hotel, before the payments are revealed and the winners are announced

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The story of how name changes in a Jewish family tested the research skills of the probate detectives, and ultimately led to an emotional family reunion. The search is also on for heirs to the six-figure estate of a talented linguist as the team attempts to trace his long-lost relatives. Plus, details of unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be found

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Spider monkey Mary is 50 and has had 19 children, and the keepers worry that another pregnancy could be risky, so they introduce younger female Kiara to the group in the hope that Mary's long-term partner Popoyan will develop a relationship with her instead. The staff also hope to breed dominant female giant otter Icana with new arrival Diego and plan to bring two feuding sloths closer together, while setting up a speed-dating event for three-legged chameleon Tripod

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Rebecca battle to get their chateau looking tip top for a tour operator who may potentially want to put it on his books for visitors to the nearby Le Mans motor racing track. Motorcycle-loving couple Martin and Kim continue their roller coaster search for a dream chateau. The clock is ticking for Fiona as she has 24 hours to prepare for her first ever wedding, and ensure that her 12-acre grounds are picture perfect for the big day. Karen, Paul and their two children need to make their chateau attractive to guests. Their latest plan is to restore a disused hen house, but tensions rise when Paul doesn't meet Karen's standards

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Identical twins Nik and Jon Daughtry build two near-identical houses next door to each other in Sheffield, paying tribute to the city's industrial past. Their plans are incredibly ambitious, not only do they want to build the two houses for the price of one, but the materials and finish have to be absolutely perfect. With a tight budget, it's an almighty stretch from the start. As delays, overages and imperfections hit, it's clear the twins' intuitive and incredibly close relationship will be tested like never before

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Surrey with Chris and Gemma, who are aiming to build an Arts and Crafts-inspired home of grand proportions. But their determination to do most of the work themselves while juggling a family means progress grinds to a halt

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets David and Jenny, who have a dream to live in a modern house in Hawthorn, a suburb in Melbourne. But their daring plan to create a three-storey home pushes their stress levels and finances to the limit

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three young men from different walks of life are brought in to the A&E department of St George's Hospital in London, including 17-year-old Shea, who has serious stab wounds and is sent to a high dependency unit. Fellow 17-year-old Bob arrives after impaling his leg on a fence while taking a shortcut home from his boys' school, and 33-year-old James is brought in by ambulance after fainting at home

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by guests Kathy Burke and Kevin Bridges. Susie Dent is assisted in Dictionary Corner by Joe Lycett, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little help from Nick Helm

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Surrey with Chris and Gemma, who are aiming to build an Arts and Crafts-inspired home of grand proportions. But their determination to do most of the work themselves while juggling a family means progress grinds to a halt

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets David and Jenny, who have a dream to live in a modern house in Hawthorn, a suburb in Melbourne. But their daring plan to create a three-storey home pushes their stress levels and finances to the limit

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team revisits favourite investigations, including Kate Quilton's visit to the world's largest producer of jelly beans to find out what goes into the sweets. Matt Tebbutt attempts to find out why tinned anchovies taste so salty, and Jimmy Doherty heads to a Mauritius distillery as he explores the secrets of rum

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp is taught how to bind books by expert Isabelle Ting, and reveals how to create a notebook from leftover materials including leather offcuts, recycled leather coats and decorative paper

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman meets Justine Noades and Ashley Pollak, who are so fond of Ibiza that they've visited the Greek party island eight times, and even got married there. Having put their night-clubbing days behind them, the pair now plan to start a family, and are seeking their dream holiday home, so Ben shows them five properties tailored to their budget of £270,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien turns tank parts into garden furniture, while Henry Cole takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien converts a lantern into a stylish table and Henry Cole changes a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp as the pair visit a private railway

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives receive a case from America with a strong showbusiness connection, and must trace the relatives of a woman who left the UK as a child in 1936. Plus, the story of a man adopted as a boy leads to an exotic tale of life on the island of Bermuda

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A new expat leaves England to start a boot camp business on the beach in Spain, and British owners of a French vineyard face a season that will make or break them

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Tower View Hotel, in Blackpool, where owner Jennifer McGirr is joined by friend Diana Carnegie. Daniel Walker and George Gillespie find some unwanted guests in the bathroom and trouble arises at breakfast when some of the guests try ordering off-menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit Church Spa in East Markham in Nottinghamshire on their second evening, where proprietor Daniel Walker and family friend George Gillespie hope to wow their guests with their relaxing facilities. Steve and Lisa Tindall seem impressed, but Jennifer and Diana Carnegie question the high price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third hosts of the week are Steve and Lisa Tindall, who own the King's Head Inn in Rye, East Sussex, where they hope their guests will fall for their boutique B&B. They get into the swing of things with a round of blindfolded mini-golf, but not everyone gets a solid night's sleep afterwards

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former breakfast TV presenter Matt Arnold welcomes the contestants to The Bridge Hotel Brandon in Suffolk, and hopes that his mantra of 'beautiful beds, blissful bathrooms and brilliant breakfasts' shines through. The group takes part in a rowing competition in the afternoon, but delays at breakfast and a runny egg cause irritation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts meet for the final time to find out what they have been paid and confront their critics, and things get heated when it's time to count up the cash

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers search for the relatives of a Northamptonshire man who left a will that was discovered to be invalid. They also visit one of the beneficiaries of a £65,000 estate, and reveal some information about his ancestor's life in India

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Golden poison frog Pablo has lost interest in his partner Maria, so 'bad boy' El Loco is brought in to shake things up. In the chimpanzee habitat, there is a power struggle for the role of dominant female, and baby porcupine Marion fends off the meerkat mob

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Rebecca receive a booking and prepare for the imminent arrival of their first paying guests. Martin and Kim take their chateau search up a gear and view the last property on their list. Paul and Karen embark on creating a cinema room in their barn, but the building looks set to collapse and their hopes of breaking even start to crumble. Angel helps to cushion the blow by upholstering a family heirloom

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows a project to renovate and extend a classic 1920s cinema in Thorne, South Yorkshire. With little knowledge of restoration, Gwyn and Kate boldly set about turning the dilapidated building into a family home using masses of concrete after taking advice from an architect friend. The couple are hoping to create a hydraulic glass wall that opens up one side of the house to form a UV-filtered canopy, and there are also plans for a white roof extension that resembles a diving board

programme.title: Coroner
programme.desc: Canadian crime drama based on MR Hall's series of novels. Coroner Jenny Cooper is called to investigate the death of a teen in a youth detention centre. Her own life is upended by the untimely death of her husband and she learns to navigate raising a teenage son on her own. Serinda Swan stars

programme.title: Secret Bridesmaids' Business
programme.desc: Saskia and Alex go to extreme lengths to conceal damning lies from the past while Olivia wrestles with her life-threatening secret and Saskia seeks out comfort. Drama, starring Abbie Cornish, Katie McGrath and Georgina Haig as three friends brought closer together by a series of shocking revelations

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 79-year-old woman is airlifted to hospital after being involved in a road traffic collision, with consultants concerned that she may have life-threatening internal bleeding. Meanwhile, 80-year-old Mohammad has come to A&E after falling at home during a family Eid celebratory feast, and 56-year-old Russ is brought in after falling from a ladder while cutting back some trees at work

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by comedians Katherine Ryan and Rob Beckett. Comedian Greg Davies is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: When multiple patients begin to show similar alarming symptoms, Sharpe and Bloom scramble to figure out the issue's root. Max works to get a child with a rare muscular disease into the country's only clinical trial that could extend his life. Medical drama, starring Ryan Eggold

programme.title: Coroner
programme.desc: Canadian crime drama based on MR Hall's series of novels. Coroner Jenny Cooper is called to investigate the death of a teen in a youth detention centre. Her own life is upended by the untimely death of her husband and she learns to navigate raising a teenage son on her own. Serinda Swan stars

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of previous investigations from the programme delving into the secrets behind mass-produced food. Jimmy Doherty heads to Kenya to find out what makes one tea more expensive than another, before meeting a taster for one of the UK's largest tea producers. Matt Tebbutt braves the icy waters of the North Sea to join divers harvesting scallops by hand from the sea bed, and Kate Quilton is in Israel to learn why some limes are much juicier than others

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie teams up with cider-maker Andy Thomstone to make a winter version of a popular drink, and demonstrates how to create the Italian favourite limoncello using leftover lemons

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lesley and Phil Ward from north Wales plan to embark on the next chapter of their lives, and hope a full-time move to La Marina Alta on the Costa Blanca in Spain. The pair have a £125,000 budget in mind, and Scarlette Douglas is on hand to show them five properties that might fit the bill, including a home by the beach and a townhouse

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo restores a pair of designer chairs, while Henry Cole breathes new life into a decrepit garden table, transforming it into a colourful and valuable new one

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn an old, unloved filing cabinet into a valuable and desirable storage unit and convert a Bakelite voltmeter into a retro Bluetooth speaker

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The race is on to find heirs to the £100,000 estate of a woman from Southsea, and the search uncovers the romantic story of a husband and wife who met while serving in the Navy. Plus, an heir seeks to learn more about her aunts and uncles and makes a poignant discovery about her mother's twin brother. The programme also provides details of other unclaimed estates

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The end of the season approaches, and after years of disappointing yields the British owners of a French vineyard begin their harvest, hoping to see their fortunes reversed. Meanwhile, decision time arrives at a British-run boot camp in Spain and the owners of a scuba diving centre on the Costa del Sol make a dramatic announcement

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Wisteria House, Hampshire, where owner Jane Faulkner invites guests to her establishment. Later, the group members try their hands at milking a cow, while 25-year-old Zak causes surprises when he reveals his years of hospitality experience

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit The Living Room B&B in Bournemouth on their second night, which is co-owned by 25-year-old Zak Busby-Pelekanou. While at the establishment, Mike and Charlie have a hairy issue, and Jane and Amy encounter a spider, before getting to grips with an ambitious breakfast menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Valley Hotel in Anglesey, where owner Mike Lewis hopes his five-star pedigree will help him reap dividends in the competition. After a visit to a local RNLI station, guests Jane and Amy are left feeling sluggish when they spot an unwanted visitor in their room, and their criticism leaves the hosts looking for answers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Elvira Barratt welcome the contestants to Barratt's at Ty'n Rhyl, Denbighshire, but the group has an unpleasant experience on arrival. The guests take part in an activity at a local cookery school where they are taught the art of preparing and cooking sheep's testicles. However, come feedback time, the hosts are left feeling distraught

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet to find out who earned the most and have it out with their rivals. Jane and Amy kick off proceedings with a gripe over David and Elvira's coffee criticism, yet are confronted themselves by Zak, who has a score to settle. Mike and Charlie of the Valley Hotel want to get to the bottom of an unwanted intruder during their stay at Wisteria House, and the ladies are challenged again regarding their cleanliness comments. Emotions run high as the cash is counted and the winner is revealed

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The team investigates the unclaimed estate of a Nottinghamshire woman, but are unsure of its value, and another search reveals the fascinating story of working life on Britain's waterways. Plus, details of more unclaimed windfalls

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Eastern black rhino Zuri gives birth to baby Ike, and sun bears Toni and Millie are having relationship issues. Meanwhile, Bactrian camel Shan-shan is under the weather, so the Chester keepers are trying to work out how to help her

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Anna and Edward host another big wedding, but Edward is put out of action and with the food delayed and the weather threatening to take a turn for the worse, Anna has to work hard to pull it off without a hitch. Dick visits Tim and Rebecca as they run into structural problems on their second B&B guest suite. With only one guest room up and running, they're in a race against time to double their income by the end of the summer season. Paul tackles a leaking outbuilding to finish the cinema room, while Karen does all she can to bag their first wedding booking

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Devon to meet Kevin McCabe, a man who wanted to build one of the biggest houses the programme has ever seen. Finally, seven years after it began, the herculean task of building this giant cob castle is complete. Has it proven to be the great home he originally intended, and does it prove as he hoped that cob is a viable material for contemporary architecture?

programme.title: Ice Cold Murders: Rocco Schiavone
programme.desc: The unorthodox police detective and his team are called to investigate the case of a dead woman found hanging from a chandelier. Everything suggests suicide, though something doesn't feel right to Rocco and he considers that if it is a set-up, who might have gone to such elaborate lengths to stage it. Crime thriller, with Marco Giallini. In Italian

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirty-year-old Chris is admitted after sustaining a potentially life-threatening wound during a game with his friend Jack, who fired the inside shaft of a ballpoint pen into his abdomen using an air rifle. College student Lennon has injured his head after jumping into a door frame while kicking a balloon, while 74-year-old Stafford arrives at A&E because his internal cardiac defibrillator keeps shocking him

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Long-distance lorry driver Neil, 52, has fallen backwards off a ramp onto a concrete floor and is sent for an urgent CT scan, the results of which are a shock. Also receiving treatments are 29-year-old Ben, who has dislocated his shoulder in a cycling accident, and Wendy, 73, who is in resus with a severe open ankle fracture after falling at home

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by comedians Katherine Ryan and Rob Beckett. Comedian Greg Davies is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers

College student Lennon has injured his head after jumping into a door frame while kicking a balloon, while 74-year-old Stafford arrives at A&E because his internal cardiac defibrillator keeps shocking him

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of favourite investigations, including Jimmy Doherty heading to southern Italy to find out where the flavour comes from in his Earl Grey tea. Kate Quilton wants to know if it is true that oysters can be eaten all year round, or whether they should be avoided in the summer months, while Matt Tebbutt is in Cornwall to learn what gives clotted cream its distinctive yellow colour

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Timber Kings
programme.desc: Beat creates a western-style guesthouse for his cowboy-loving clients. Elsewhere, Andre and Joel build an archway to rope in the tourists

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: When the guys visit a woman overwhelmed by an incredible inheritance, she opens the doors to a secret vault

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: In the penultimate episode, an unlikely pairing enrages bidders in Birmingham, Linda is determined to keep her game face on despite Dan's jibes, and Nat and John are at each other's throats. Sean Kelly hosts

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Sparks are flying in Royston, Hertfordshire, and not just amongst the bidders as the contents of a bin goes up in smoke, and a mystery object under tarp causes a cat-fight. Sean's flying skills are put to the test and Nat and John take a gamble on a double unit - but will it pay off?

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike makes a mistake in Indiana, and while the guys get a warm reception from a carnie collector in Ohio, the pick brings an unexpected sting

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys visit a man who has not thrown away anything since the 1950s, and is not willing to start now. And he also has a penchant for swimming trunks

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Driving up Madagascar's eastern coast at the tail end of the rainy season is enough to test the best of friendships, but comedy actor Angus Deayton and broadcaster Mariella Frostrup are game enough to take on the challenge, and good enough friends to poke fun at each other along the way. The route has numerous river crossings and the duo face a variety of broken bridges and flimsy ferries as the only way across, while the roads themselves are mired with thick mud. Progress becomes increasingly painful and they are finally forced to spend a night in the car

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts an extended version of the quiz with a difference, with guests Sheila Hancock, Jimmy Carr and Jeremy Clarkson joining regular panellist Alan Davies, focusing on military matters

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets to grips with a Peel P50, the smallest car to go into production, and finds a surprising number of uses for it. James May experiences the luxury of the Rolls-Royce Phantom Drophead Coupe convertible, and the Stig puts the Ferrari 599 GTB Fiorano through its paces on the test track. The Rolling Stones' Ronnie Wood is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef heads for the region of Extremadura, famous for paprika and Iberico ham. He then concludes his exploration of the country by driving south in his camper van to the city of Seville - the capital of tapas and flamenco

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Driving up Madagascar's eastern coast at the tail end of the rainy season is enough to test the best of friendships, but comedy actor Angus Deayton and broadcaster Mariella Frostrup are game enough to take on the challenge, and good enough friends to poke fun at each other along the way. The route has numerous river crossings and the duo face a variety of broken bridges and flimsy ferries as the only way across, while the roads themselves are mired with thick mud. Progress becomes increasingly painful and they are finally forced to spend a night in the car

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.desc: Twenty Twelve co-stars Hugh Bonneville and Jessica Hynes pair up again to go on a road trip through the former Soviet republic of Georgia. Driving from the border with Azerbaijan to the Black Sea, Jessica soon discovers her spirit of adventure is causing Hugh a few moments of high anxiety, but there is worse to come when they find themselves stuck on a high mountain pass in bad weather and he has to overcome his vertigo. While the drive is a challenge all the way, the country and its people delight and surprise them at every turn

programme.title: Rick Stein's Far Eastern Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a culinary journey by sea and land to explore the Far East's most diverse food cultures. He begins in Cambodia, where tourists are beginning to return after the fall of the Khmer Rouge regime. As well as discovering the joys of freshly caught swimmer crabs with green peppercorns, he learns how to cook the country's most famous dish - fish amok - and samples lok lak, made with marinated beef. He also visits the Angkor Wat temple and joins a rice barge to travel down the Mekong River

programme.desc: Comedians Phill Jupitus and Marcus Brigstocke head to Bolivia for a high-altitude trip, first driving along the infamous 'Road of Death', only to find themselves deep in bandit country. Disorientation takes them by surprise as they have to navigate Salar de Uyuni - a salt lake so vast that Buzz Aldrin could see it from space. Their goal is the ancient mountain city of Potosi, whose silver mines financed the Spanish empire in the 16th century and which is almost 5,000 metres above sea level

programme.desc: Chief Curator at Historic Royal Palaces, Dr Tracy Borman travels to Germany to uncover Nazi Party leader Adolf Hitler's abusive childhood and artistic aspirations. programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to venture beyond Europe as he travels through the Holy Land. He begins in Israel, learning about the Baha'i faith and how it spread to Britain in the Edwardian era, hearing the story of the origins of Tel Aviv, and visiting the Christian, Jewish and Muslim quarters of the capital Jerusalem. Michael then crosses the separation barrier between Jerusalem and the West Bank in the company of his Palestinian guide and meets the embroiderers of the Arab Women's Union in Bethlehem, before finishing his journey in the Negev desert

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Ightham Mote, a medieval manor near Sevenoaks. Objects brought in to be valued include a set of hold and porcelain false teeth from the 1850s, which triggers a grim look back at the history of dentures. Plus, Walter Scott's walking cane, and two cricket bats dating back to the 18th century

programme.title: Steam Train Britain

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo follows in the footsteps of Edwardian travellers to trace a route recommended in his Bradshaw's guide from the heart of France to the Mediterranean coast. His journey begins in Lyon, where he learns how the city got its gastronomic reputation, and takes instruction from a leading chef on making an omelette. In Avignon, he samples a glass of the region's famous wine Chateauneuf-du-Pape, before his journey ends at the Mediterranean port of Marseille, where he joins a pilot boat as it leads a supertanker to its berth

programme.title: VE Day: Countdown to Peace
programme.desc: This compelling documentary celebrates the approaching 75th Anniversary of freedom and peace in Europe

programme.desc: Hitler's launch of his great counter-offensive against the unsuspecting Allies in the Ardennes. This was the Nazis' last gamble in the West to turn the tide of the war

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his trusty 1913 Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo travels east through the Balkans along the most exotic section of the route taken by the Orient Express. His journey begins in Sofia, capital of Bulgaria, and includes stops in the ancient city of Plovdiv, the region of Rumelia, and former capital of the Ottoman Empire Edirne. Arriving in Istanbul, Michael takes a trip on the Bosphorus - a much-coveted stretch of water - finds out about Turkish delight and travels along the Marmaray metro line, which connects European and Asian Istanbul via a tunnel beneath the Bosphorus
